174	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Proj.awayι` chart-evaluation infrastructure
175	
176	The blueprint `chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` (iter-195/iter-196 analogist recipe
177	`lane-e-proj-appiso-pivot`, ANALOGUE_FOUND) identifies two project-local Mathlib lemmas
178	that, together, evaluate `((Proj.awayι 𝒜 f).appIso ⊤).inv` on the canonical generator
179	`isLocElem ∈ Away 𝒜 f`. The two lemmas mirror the Mathlib pattern
180	`IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean:560-564`)
181	applied to the `Proj`-side basic-open factorisation.
182	
183	Each declaration below is project-local (general-purpose graded ring `𝒜 : ℕ → σ`); none
184	mention `kbar` or `projectiveLineBarGrading`. They are placed here as supplements
185	because they have no upstream Mathlib home (yet). -/
186	
187	/-- Project-local supplement: the preimage of `Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f` under
188	`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm` is `⊤`. Combines `Proj.opensRange_awayι` with
189	`Scheme.Hom.preimage_opensRange`. -/
190	lemma Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self
191	    {σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
192	    (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
193	    Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm ⁻¹ᵁ Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f = ⊤ := by
194	  rw [← Proj.opensRange_awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm, Scheme.Hom.preimage_opensRange]
195	
196	/-- Project-local supplement: `Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm` factors as
197	`Spec.map (basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫ (isAffineOpen_basicOpen ...).fromSpec`.
198	
199	This is the "iter-195 analogist recipe step (i)" `Proj.awayι_isoSpec_compat` from
200	`analogies/lane-e-proj-appiso-pivot.md`. It is the bridge that lets us transport
201	Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self` (which is about `Γ(X, U)`, not the
202	project's `Away 𝒜 f`) into a closed-form for `(Proj.awayι).app`. -/
203	lemma Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec
204	    {σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
205	    (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
206	    Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm =
207	      Spec.map (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
208	      (Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).fromSpec := by
209	  rw [Proj.awayι, IsAffineOpen.fromSpec, ← Category.assoc]
210	  congr 1
211	  -- Goal: `basicOpenIsoSpec.inv = Spec.map(basicOpenIsoAway.inv) ≫ isoSpec.inv`.
212	  -- Repackage RHS as the `inv` of a single `Iso.trans` so we can apply `Iso.ext`
213	  -- on the `.hom` side, where the `basicOpenIsoSpec_hom`/`basicOpenToSpec`/
214	  -- `isoSpec_hom = toSpecΓ` chain composes definitionally.
215	  rw [show (Spec.map (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
216	       (Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).isoSpec.inv) =
217	       ((Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).isoSpec ≪≫
218	         Scheme.Spec.mapIso (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).op).inv from rfl]
219	  congr 1
220	  apply Iso.ext
221	  rw [Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom, Proj.basicOpenToSpec, Iso.trans_hom,
222	      IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom]
223	  rfl
224	
225	/-- **Iter-197 Lane E (blueprint `lem:basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top`).**
226	Closed-form for `(basicOpenIsoSpec).inv.app ⊤`: the inverse of the canonical
227	scheme iso `Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec`, evaluated on global sections, factors as
228	`topIso.hom ≫ basicOpenIsoAway.inv ≫ (ΓSpecIso _).inv`.
229	
230	Proven by `cancel_mono` on the iso `basicOpenIsoSpec.hom.appTop` (an iso because
231	its closed form via `basicOpenToSpec_app_top` is a composite of three isos:
232	`ΓSpecIso.hom`, `basicOpenIsoAway.hom`, `topIso.inv`). After cancellation both
233	sides reduce to `𝟙 Γ(basicOpen 𝒜 f, ⊤)` by `Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc` triples. -/
234	lemma Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top
235	    {σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
236	    (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
237	    (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv.app ⊤ =
238	      (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).topIso.hom ≫
239	      (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
240	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv := by
241	  set β := Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm with hβ
242	  set α := Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm with hα
243	  have hβh : Scheme.Hom.appTop β.hom =
244	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).hom ≫ α.hom ≫ (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).topIso.inv := by
245	    change Scheme.Hom.appTop (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom = _
246	    rw [Scheme.Hom.appTop, Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom, Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top,
247	        Proj.basicOpenIsoAway_hom]
248	  haveI : IsIso (Scheme.Hom.appTop β.hom) := by rw [hβh]; infer_instance
249	  change Scheme.Hom.appTop β.inv = _
250	  apply (cancel_mono (Scheme.Hom.appTop β.hom)).mp
251	  rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, β.hom_inv_id, Scheme.Hom.id_appTop, hβh]
252	  rw [Category.assoc, Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc, α.inv_hom_id_assoc,
253	      Iso.hom_inv_id]
254	
255	/-- **Iter-197 Lane E (blueprint `lem:awayι_app_basicOpen`).** Section-level
256	closed-form for `(Proj.awayι 𝒜 f).app (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f)`: the canonical
257	section pullback factors as `basicOpenIsoAway.inv ≫ (ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫
258	(Spec _).presheaf.map (eqToHom <preimage = ⊤>).op`. Port of Mathlib's
259	`IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self` (`AffineScheme.lean:560-564`) to the
260	`Proj.awayι` direction.
261	
262	Proven via the definitional unfolding `awayι = basicOpenIsoSpec.inv ≫ ι`
263	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:189-190`), then
264	`Scheme.Hom.comp_app` + `Scheme.Opens.ι_app_self` + `Scheme.Hom.app_eq` on
265	the `ι_preimage_self` equation + the iter-197 named helper
266	`Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top`, then `aesop_cat` to collapse the
267	residual `eqToHom` cascade. -/
268	lemma Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen
269	    {σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
270	    (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
271	    (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm).app (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f) =
272	      (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
273	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫
274	      (Spec _).presheaf.map
275	        (eqToHom (Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self 𝒜 f f_deg hm)).op := by
276	  change ((Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫ (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).ι).app
277	    (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f) = _
278	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_app, Scheme.Opens.ι_app_self,
279	      Scheme.Hom.app_eq (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv
280	        ((Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).ι_preimage_self),
281	      Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top]
282	  aesop_cat
283	
284	/-- **Iter-197 Lane E (blueprint `lem:awayι_appIso_top_inv_apply_isLocElem`).**
285	Closed form for the inverse of `(Proj.awayι 𝒜 f).appIso ⊤`: the section-level
286	inverse iso factors as `(ΓSpecIso _).hom ≫ basicOpenIsoAway.hom ≫
287	presheaf.map(eqToHom <opensRange = basicOpen>).op`.
288	
289	Proven by computing `((Proj.awayι 𝒜 f).appIso ⊤).hom` via Mathlib's
290	`Scheme.Hom.appIso_hom` + our `Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen`, then inverting via
291	`Iso.inv_eq_inv`. After cancellation of the `eqToHom` cascade (which collapses
292	to `𝟙` on `Γ(Spec, ⊤)`), only the three iso-components survive: the inverse of
293	`basicOpenIsoAway` and `ΓSpecIso`, plus the eqToHom presheaf-map adjustment. -/
294	lemma Proj.awayι_appIso_top_inv
295	    {σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
296	    (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
297	    ((Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm).appIso ⊤).inv =
298	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).hom ≫ (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom ≫
299	        (Proj 𝒜).presheaf.map
300	          (eqToHom ((Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm)).trans
301	            (Proj.opensRange_awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm))).op := by
302	  -- Compute via the .hom side: show `RHS ≫ appIso.hom = 𝟙`.
303	  refine Eq.symm ?_
304	  refine (Iso.comp_hom_eq_id _).mp ?_
305	  rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_hom,
306	      Scheme.Hom.app_eq (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm)
307	        ((Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm)).trans
308	          (Proj.opensRange_awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm)),
309	      Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen]
310	  simp only [Category.assoc]
311	  -- Combine adjacent presheaf.map(eqToHom).op pairs via Functor.map_comp + eqToHom_trans.
312	  slice_lhs 7 8 => rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, eqToHom_trans]
313	  slice_lhs 3 4 => rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, eqToHom_trans]
314	  -- Collapse the combined eqToHoms to identities.
315	  simp only [eqToHom_refl, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.id_comp]
316	  -- Two more adjacent Spec.presheaf.map(eqToHom).op factors at positions 5+6.
317	  slice_lhs 5 6 => rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, eqToHom_trans]
318	  simp only [eqToHom_refl, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.id_comp,
319	             Category.comp_id]
320	  -- Final residual: cancel `basicOpenIsoAway.hom ≫ basicOpenIsoAway.inv` and the ΓSpecIso pair.
321	  rw [(Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom_inv_id_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id]
322	
323	/-! ### Iter-193 Lane E refactor — `IsOpenImmersion.lift_uniq` route
324	
325	The substantive content of `iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval` (the chart-1 `Proj.appIso`
326	evaluation `isLocElem ↦ 1`) repeatedly STUCK across iter-188/189/190/191 on the
327	`Proj.appIso` evaluation step. Iter-193 reroutes via `IsOpenImmersion.lift_uniq`:
328	since `iotaGm_r_1 = IsOpenImmersion.lift (Proj.awayι X_1) onePt.left h_range` by
329	definition, *any* `Spec.map(F)` satisfying the same lift identity must equal
330	`iotaGm_r_1`. So if we can build an explicit ring map
331	`kbarChart1Ring : Away 𝒜 X_1 →+* kbar`
332	sending `isLocElem ↦ 1` and prove `Spec.map kbarChart1Ring ≫ Proj.awayι X_1 =
333	onePt.left`, then `iotaGm_r_1 = Spec.map kbarChart1Ring` (by `lift_uniq`), and
334	the whole chart-1 evaluation reduces to a ring-map identity on `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`. -/
335	
336	/-- **Iter-193 Lane E (helper).** The chart-`1` evaluation ring map at the `k̄`-point
337	`[1:1]`: `Away 𝒜 X_1 →+* k̄` sending the chart-`1` affine coordinate
338	`isLocElem = [X_0/X_1]` to `1 ∈ k̄`.
339	
340	Defined as the composition
341	`Away 𝒜 X_1 →+* MvPolynomial Unit k̄ →+* k̄`
342	where the first map is the chart-`1` ring iso forward direction
343	(`homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar 1`, sending `isLocElem ↦ X () = u`)
344	and the second is `MvPolynomial.eval (fun _ ↦ 1)` (evaluation of the chart-`1`
345	affine coordinate `u` at `λ = 1`). -/
346	private noncomputable def kbarChart1Ring (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
347	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
348	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
349	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1) →+*
350	      kbar :=
351	  (MvPolynomial.eval (fun _ : Unit => (1 : kbar))).comp
352	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar 1)
353

9	/-!
10	# The Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (A.4.d)
11	
12	This file is the **A.4.d** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages Milne's
14	*Abelian Varieties* §III.6 Proposition 6.1: for a complete nonsingular curve
15	`C` of genus `g > 0` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked
16	`k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the Abel–Jacobi morphism
17	`ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` is the **Albanese morphism** of the pointed curve
18	`(C, P₀)`: any pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety with
19	`φ(P₀) = η_A` factors uniquely as `φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}` for a homomorphism of
20	group schemes `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.
21	
22	The chapter commits to the **symmetric-power route** (cube-free): the
23	symmetric assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` factors through
24	`Sym^g C`, the resulting morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` descends through
25	the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` to a rational map
26	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, and Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (`extend_to_av` in the sibling
27	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c) promotes it to a regular
28	morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The group-homomorphism property of `ψ` then
29	follows from `ψ(η_J) = η_A` together with Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a
30	consequence of `thm:rigidity_lemma`).
31	
32	## Status (iter-177 Lane 7 file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-177 Lane 7** file-skeleton. Each of the six
35	blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended* substantive type
36	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
38	
39	The 6 pinned declarations are:
40	
41	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.abelJacobi` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the
42	   regular morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
43	   `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism`. Encoded against the file-internal
44	   placeholder `Pic0.jacobianScheme` for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (the A.3 upstream
45	   has not yet been split out; see §0 below).
46	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.SymmetricPower` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC) —
47	   the `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C` of the curve `C`, as a `k̄`-scheme.
48	   Body gated on iter-178+ `SymmetricPower.lean` substrate (Milne III.3
49	   Proposition 3.1; Mumford 1970 II.7 / III.11 affine-and-glue recipe).
50	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerAVMap` (noncomputable def, ~5 LOC) —
51	   the symmetrised morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` produced by
52	   `lem:symmetric_product_av_map` from an input `φ : C ⟶ A`.
53	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerToJacobian` (noncomputable def,
54	   ~5 LOC) — the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
55	   `lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian`.
56	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.descentThroughBirationalSigma` (theorem,
57	   ~10 LOC) — the descent of `Sym^g φ` to a regular morphism
58	   `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` via Milne Theorem 3.2.
59	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.albanese_universal_property` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
60	   the headline UP theorem (Milne Proposition III.6.1).
61	
62	## File-internal placeholder for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
63	
64	The A.3 row of Route A (the identity-component refinement of the Picard
65	scheme together with its degree map) has not yet been split out into a
66	dedicated chapter or `.lean` file at the time of this file-skeleton
67	landing. We supply a **single helper** here: a `Pic0.Bundle` structure
68	bundling the underlying scheme together with its four abelian-variety
69	instances (`GrpObj`, `IsProper`, `Smooth`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`),
70	plus a typed-`sorry` carrier `Pic0.bundle C` and a derived definition
71	`Pic0.jacobianScheme C := (bundle C).scheme`. The four instances on
72	`jacobianScheme C` are derived from the bundle's fields (no extra
73	`sorry`). Once the A.3 chapter materialises this placeholder collapses to
74	a one-line re-export.
75	
76	Helper budget = 1: the single helper carrying a `sorry` is `Pic0.bundle`.
77	
78	## Note on type expressivity
79	
80	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
81	each pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
82	
83	- `abelJacobi C P₀ : C ⟶ jacobianScheme C` — a genuine morphism into the
84	  Pic⁰ scheme (not `𝟙 C` or any tautology).
85	- `SymmetricPower C g : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` — a `k̄`-scheme, not `C`
86	  itself or `𝟙_`.
87	- `symmetricPowerAVMap C g φ : SymmetricPower C g ⟶ A` — a morphism into
88	  the abelian variety `A`.
89	- `symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ g : SymmetricPower C g ⟶ jacobianScheme C`
90	  — a morphism into Pic⁰.
91	- `descentThroughBirationalSigma` — `∃!` existence-and-uniqueness of
92	  `ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A` satisfying
93	  `symmetricPowerAVMap C g φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ g ≫ ψ`.
94	- `albanese_universal_property` — `∃!` existence-and-uniqueness of
95	  `ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A` satisfying `φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ`.
96	
97	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
98	(a morphism into the Pic⁰ scheme, a unique factorisation through the
99	Abel–Jacobi map, …); no `Iso.refl _` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` /
100	empty-content `proof_wanted` placeholders are used.
101	
102	## Abelian-variety conventions
103	
104	Following the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
105	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
106	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
107	
108	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
109	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
110	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
111	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
112	
113	A **smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over `k̄`** carries:
114	
115	- `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]`,
116	- `[IsProper C.hom]`,
117	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]`.
118	
119	These instances are supplied by `inferInstance` at the call site.
120	
121	## References
122	
123	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex` (~830 LOC,
124	6 pins). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §III.6 Proposition 6.1, p. 104;
125	§III.3 Proposition 3.1 (symmetric power), p. 94; §III.5 Theorem 5.1(a)
126	(birationality of `f^{(g)}`), p. 101 (`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`).
127	-/

211	/-! ## §1. The Abel–Jacobi morphism at a marked point
212	
213	The first sub-lemma packages the construction and elementary regularity
214	of the Abel–Jacobi morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` at the basepoint
215	`P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`. On `k̄`-points,
216	`ι_{P₀}(Q) = [𝓞_C(Q − P₀)] ∈ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}(k̄)`; at the basepoint
217	itself `ι_{P₀}(P₀) = η_J`.
218	
219	This is the content gated on A.3 (existence of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` as a
220	representable group scheme); the morphism is the moduli classifier of
221	the rigidified diagonal correspondence
222	`𝓛^{P₀} = 𝓞_{C × C}(Δ − {P₀} × C − C × {P₀})` on `C × C`, taken as a
223	relative degree-zero line bundle on `C × C` over the second factor.
224	
225	Blueprint reference: `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism` (Milne, *Abelian
226	Varieties*, Proposition III.6.1 and Summary III.6.11). -/
227	
228	/-- **The Abel–Jacobi morphism at the marked basepoint `P₀`.**
229	
230	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an
231	algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked `k̄`-point
232	`P₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ C`, the **Abel–Jacobi morphism** is the unique regular
233	morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` characterised on `T`-points by the
234	formula `(1 × ι_{P₀})^* 𝓜^{P₀} ≈ 𝓛^{P₀}`, where `𝓜^{P₀}` is the
235	Poincaré–Picard correspondence on `C × Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` and
236	`𝓛^{P₀} = 𝓞_{C × C}(Δ − {P₀} × C − C × {P₀})`.
237	
238	iter-178+: body constructed by applying the moduli interpretation of
239	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (`def:pic_scheme` together with the identity-component
240	refinement A.3) to the relative degree-zero line bundle `𝓛^{P₀}` on
241	`C × C → C` along the second projection. Yoneda then produces the
242	unique classifying morphism `C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. The basepoint
243	condition `P₀ ≫ abelJacobi C P₀ = η[Pic⁰_{C/k̄}]` (`lem:abel_jacobi_morphism`)
244	holds because restriction of `𝓛^{P₀}` to `C × {P₀}` is the trivial
245	line bundle `𝓞_C(P₀ − P₀) ≅ 𝓞_C` whose moduli class is `η_J`.
246	
247	For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
248	noncomputable def abelJacobi (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C) :
249	    C ⟶ jacobianScheme C :=

252	/-! ## §2. The `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C`
253	
254	The `g`-th symmetric power of `C` is the quotient of the `g`-fold
255	Cartesian power `C^g` by the natural permutation action of the
256	symmetric group `S_g`. Mathlib `b80f227` has no formalised symmetric
257	power of a scheme — only `Sym` / `SymmetricPower` for types and
258	modules — so the scheme-theoretic construction must be supplied
259	project-side, following Milne's recipe of §III.3 Proposition 3.1: on
260	each open affine `U = Spec A ⊆ C` form `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`, then glue
261	along the standard open-cover patching (Mumford 1970 §II.7 / §III.11).
262	
263	This sub-build is a non-trivial dependency of A.4.d that the prover
264	must absorb before the body of `symmetricPowerAVMap` /
265	`symmetricPowerToJacobian` closes; the carrier here records the
266	substantive *target* `Sym^g C : Over (Spec k̄)` with a typed `sorry`
267	body, deferring the affine-and-glue construction to iter-178+
268	`Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean`.
269	
270	Blueprint reference: `def:symmetric_power_curve` (Milne, *Abelian
271	Varieties*, §III.3, Proposition 3.1, p. 94). -/
272	
273	/-- **The `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C`** of a complete nonsingular
274	curve `C` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, as a `k̄`-scheme.
275	
276	Milne III.3 Proposition 3.1 constructs `Sym^g C` as the quotient of
277	`C^g` by the natural permutation action of the symmetric group `S_g`:
278	on each open affine `U = Spec A ⊆ C`, form `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`, then
279	glue along the standard open-cover patching. The construction comes
280	equipped with a canonical finite surjective symmetric morphism
281	`π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C` characterised by the universal property that
282	every `S_g`-symmetric morphism `C^g ⟶ T` factors uniquely through `π`.
283	
284	This pin records the substantive *target* scheme `Sym^g C`; the
285	projection `π` and its universal property are recorded as helper API
286	in the iter-178+ `Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean` substrate file.
287	
288	iter-178+: body is the gluing-of-affines `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`
289	construction over an open affine cover of `C`. For the iter-177
290	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`.
291	
292	The signature mentions `C` explicitly (not via the file's `variable`
293	block) so that the declaration's first explicit argument is the curve
294	`C`, matching the call sites `SymmetricPower C g`. -/
295	noncomputable def SymmetricPower
296	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
297	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
298	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
299	    (_g : ℕ) : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
300	  sorry

302	/-! ## §3. The morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A`
303	
304	The second sub-lemma is the elementary fact that the symmetric
305	assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` from `C^g` to `A`
306	descends through `π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C`. The key point is that the
307	`g`-fold addition morphism `add_A : A^g ⟶ A` is itself `S_g`-symmetric
308	because the underlying group object `A` is commutative as a group
309	scheme; combined with the universal property of `Sym^g C`, this yields
310	the unique factorisation.
311	
312	Blueprint reference: `lem:symmetric_product_av_map` (Milne's proof of
313	III.6.1, p. 104, "Clearly this is symmetric, and so it factors through
314	`C^{(g)}`"). -/
315	
316	/-- **The symmetrised morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A`.**
317	
318	Let `φ : C ⟶ A` be a morphism into an abelian variety `A` over `k̄`.
319	The composition `add_A ∘ φ^{×g} : C^g ⟶ A^g ⟶ A` sending
320	`(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` is `S_g`-equivariant (where `S_g`
321	permutes the factors of `C^g` and acts trivially on `A`), because
322	addition in a commutative group object is order-independent. By the
323	universal property of `Sym^g C` (`def:symmetric_power_curve`) it
324	factors uniquely as `Sym^g φ ∘ π = add_A ∘ φ^{×g}`.
325	
326	iter-178+: body is the universal-property invocation on the
327	symmetric composition `add_A ∘ φ^{×g}` once the iter-178+
328	`Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean` substrate exposes the projection `π`
329	and its UP. For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
330	noncomputable def symmetricPowerAVMap (g : ℕ)
331	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
332	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
333	    (_φ : C ⟶ A) :
334	    SymmetricPower C g ⟶ A :=
335	  sorry

337	/-! ## §4. The birational morphism `Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
338	
339	The third sub-lemma is the birational identification of `Sym^g C` with
340	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. After choosing the basepoint `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the cycle
341	map `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ [𝓞_C(P₁ + ⋯ + P_g − g P₀)]` defines a regular
342	morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` which is birational by
343	Milne III Theorem 5.1(a) (the proof uses dimension count plus the
344	Riemann–Roch identification of the generic fibre as a single point on
345	a curve of genus `g`).
346	
347	Blueprint reference: `lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian` (Milne,
348	*Abelian Varieties*, Theorem III.5.1(a), p. 101). -/
349	
350	/-- **The birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`.**
351	
352	Given a basepoint `P₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ C`, the symmetrisation of the Abel–Jacobi
353	morphism `add_J ∘ ι_{P₀}^{×g} : C^g ⟶ J^g ⟶ J` descends through
354	`π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C` to a regular morphism
355	`f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` sending an effective divisor class
356	`[P₁ + ⋯ + P_g]` to `[𝓞_C(P₁ + ⋯ + P_g − g P₀)] ∈ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}(k̄)`.
357	By Milne III Theorem 5.1(a) this morphism is *birational*: it is
358	dominant with one-dimensional fibres, and its generic fibre is a single
359	`k̄`-point (the linear system `|D|` of a general degree-`g` divisor `D`
360	on a curve of genus `g` is `0`-dimensional by Riemann–Roch).
361	
362	The birationality data (a dense open subset `U ⊆ Sym^g C` mapping
363	isomorphically onto a dense open `V ⊆ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`) is recorded as
364	helper API in the iter-178+ proof body of
365	`descentThroughBirationalSigma`.
366	
367	iter-178+: body is the specialisation of `symmetricPowerAVMap` to
368	`A := jacobianScheme C` and `φ := abelJacobi C P₀`. For the iter-177
369	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
370	noncomputable def symmetricPowerToJacobian
371	    (_P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C) (g : ℕ) :
372	    SymmetricPower C g ⟶ jacobianScheme C :=
373	  sorry

375	/-! ## §5. Descent through the birational quotient
376	
377	The final auxiliary lemma packages the two-step descent that promotes
378	`Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` (after passing through the birational morphism
379	`f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`) to a regular morphism
380	`ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The descent is a rational map
381	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A` on the dense open of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` on which
382	`f^{(g)}` is an isomorphism, then promoted to a regular morphism via
383	Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (the sibling `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`).
384	This is the unique step of the proof that consumes A.4.c.
385	
386	Blueprint reference: `lem:descent_through_birational_sigma` (Milne's
387	proof of III.6.1, p. 104, "It therefore defines a rational map
388	`ψ : J → A`, which (I 3.2) shows to be a regular map"). -/
389	
390	/-- **Descent of `Sym^g φ` to a regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.**
391	
392	There is a *unique* regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that
393	`symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ (genus C) ≫ ψ`
394	(equivalently, `ψ` is the unique regular extension of the rational map
395	`Sym^g φ ∘ (f^{(g)})^{-1} : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`).
396	
397	The rational map is constructed on the dense open `V ⊆ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
398	where `f^{(g)}` is an isomorphism, then promoted to a regular morphism
399	on all of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` via Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (sibling
400	`Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` in
401	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c). Uniqueness is the
402	standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle: two regular
403	morphisms `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` agreeing on a dense open are equal.
404	
405	iter-178+: body invokes `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` on the
406	rational map produced by inverting `f^{(g)}` on its dense open of
407	isomorphism. For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed
408	`sorry`. -/
409	theorem descentThroughBirationalSigma
410	    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
411	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
412	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
413	    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
414	    ∃! (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A),
415	      symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ
416	        = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P0 (genus C) ≫ ψ := by
417	  sorry

419	/-! ## §5.5. Albanese ⇔ symmetric-power equation (connecting biconditional)
420	
421	The iter-182 structural decomposition extracts the precise *connecting
422	identity* between the Albanese-form factorisation
423	`φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ` and the symmetric-power-form factorisation
424	`symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀
425	(genus C) ≫ ψ` as a named typed-`sorry` biconditional helper. With this
426	biconditional in hand the body of `albanese_universal_property` reduces
427	to sorry-free assembly from `descentThroughBirationalSigma`. -/
428	
429	/-- **Connecting biconditional: Albanese-form equation ⇔ symmetric-power-form
430	equation.**
431	
432	For every candidate descent morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`, the Albanese-form
433	factorisation `φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ` holds iff the symmetric-power-form
434	factorisation `symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian
435	C P₀ (genus C) ≫ ψ` holds. The forward direction precomposes both sides of
436	the Albanese equation with the symmetric `g`-fold sum, then descends through
437	`π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C` and uses the additivity of `ψ` (a homomorphism of group
438	schemes). The reverse direction restricts the symmetric-power-form equation
439	along the diagonal embedding `Q ↦ (Q, P₀, …, P₀)` and uses
440	`φ(P₀) = η_A`.
441	
442	iter-200+: body uses the `Sym^g` projection π and its UP from
443	`Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean` (substrate file not yet opened),
444	together with the explicit Milne III §6 identification of `ι_{P₀}` with
445	the composite of `Q ↦ Q + (g - 1) P₀` followed by `f^{(g)}`. For the
446	iter-182 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry` — but the named
447	substantive helper exposes the precise connecting identity, allowing
448	`albanese_universal_property` to be assembled sorry-free from it. -/
449	theorem albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq
450	    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
451	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
452	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
453	    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
454	    ∀ (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A),
455	      (φ = abelJacobi C P0 ≫ ψ) ↔
456	      (symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ
457	        = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P0 (genus C) ≫ ψ) :=
458	  sorry

460	/-! ## §6. The Albanese universal property — headline theorem
461	
462	The headline result of the chapter: for every pointed morphism
463	`φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety `A` with `φ(P₀) = η_A`, there
464	exists a unique regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that
465	`φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}`. The proof follows Milne Proposition III.6.1
466	verbatim, using the four sub-lemmas above plus Milne Theorem I.3.2
467	(`extend_to_av`) and Corollary I.1.2 (a `thm:rigidity_lemma`
468	consequence).
469	
470	Blueprint reference: `thm:albanese_universal_property` (Milne,
471	*Abelian Varieties*, Proposition III.6.1, p. 104). -/
472	
473	/-- **Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` — Milne III §6
474	Proposition 6.1.**
475	
476	For a complete nonsingular curve `C` of genus `g > 0` over an
477	algebraically closed field `k̄`, a marked `k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, and
478	every pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety `A` with
479	`φ(P₀) = η_A`, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
480	`ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that `φ = ι_{P₀} ≫ ψ`.
481	
482	Proof sketch (Milne III.6.1):
483	1. **Symmetrisation.** `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` is
484	   `S_g`-symmetric (`A` commutative), so factors through
485	   `Sym^g C` as `Sym^g φ` (`lem:symmetric_product_av_map`).
486	2. **Descent to a regular morphism on `J`.** The birational morphism
487	   `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (`lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian`)
488	   exhibits `Sym^g φ ∘ (f^{(g)})^{-1}` as a rational map
489	   `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, which by Milne Theorem I.3.2 extends uniquely to
490	   a regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`
491	   (`lem:descent_through_birational_sigma`).
492	3. **Factorisation `ψ ∘ ι_{P₀} = φ`.** Use Milne's identification of
493	   `ι_{P₀}` with the composite `Q ↦ Q + (g − 1) P₀` followed by
494	   `f^{(g)}`; together with `φ(P₀) = η_A` and the additive structure
495	   of `A`, this gives `ψ(ι_{P₀}(Q)) = φ(Q)` on `k̄`-points, hence
496	   globally by the reduced-and-separated agreement principle.
497	4. **Homomorphism property.** `ψ` sends `η_J = ι_{P₀}(P₀)` to
498	   `φ(P₀) = η_A`, so by Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a `thm:rigidity_lemma`
499	   consequence) it is a homomorphism of group schemes.
500	5. **Uniqueness.** Two homomorphisms `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` with the same
501	   restriction to `ι_{P₀}(C)` agree on the `g`-fold sum
502	   `ι_{P₀}(C) + ⋯ + ι_{P₀}(C) = Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (since `f^{(g)}` is
503	   surjective), hence are equal.
504	
505	The iter-177 file-skeleton encodes only the existence-and-uniqueness
506	of the factoring morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` (matching the
507	project's `IsAlbanese` precedent in `Jacobian.lean`); the
508	group-homomorphism property is implicit in the construction. The body
509	consumes its four sub-lemma exports plus
510	`Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (A.4.c) and Milne Corollary I.1.2
511	(registered in `chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
512	
513	iter-182 update: structural decomposition lands the body as
514	sorry-free assembly from `descentThroughBirationalSigma` (§5)
515	combined with the named typed-`sorry` biconditional helper
516	`albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq` (§5.5). The remaining substantive
517	gaps (substrate files `Pic⁰` representability, `Sym^g C` construction,
518	`extend_to_av` rational-map extension, and the connecting biconditional
519	body itself) remain blocking and are deferred to iter-200+. -/
520	theorem albanese_universal_property
521	    (_hg : 0 < genus C)
522	    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
523	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
524	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
525	    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
526	    ∃! (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A), φ = abelJacobi C P0 ≫ ψ := by
527	  -- Structural decomposition (iter-182): reduce to the symmetric-power
528	  -- descent `descentThroughBirationalSigma` plus the connecting
529	  -- biconditional `albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq`. The remaining
530	  -- substantive gap is the biconditional helper (named typed-sorry); the
531	  -- proof body below is sorry-free assembly.
532	  have key := albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq C P0 φ _hφ
533	  obtain ⟨ψ, hψ_sym, huniq_sym⟩ := descentThroughBirationalSigma C P0 φ _hφ
534	  exact ⟨ψ, (key ψ).mpr hψ_sym,
535	    fun ψ' hψ' => huniq_sym ψ' ((key ψ').mp hψ')⟩
536	
537	end Pic0
538	
539	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	# Auslander–Buchsbaum formula (A.4.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.4.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula and the corollary "regular local ring
14	⟹ Cohen–Macaulay" that the sibling sub-build **A.4.a** (codim-1 extension of a
15	rational map across a codim-2 closed point on a regular projective surface)
16	consumes.
17	
18	Per STRATEGY.md L30 this row is gated downstream on A.4.a but is independently
19	startable on the Mathlib side: the algebra here is decoupled from the Albanese
20	geometry and lives entirely under `RingTheory.*`. Mathlib at the project's
21	pinned commit (`b80f227`) exposes `IsRegularLocalRing` and the categorical
22	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` on an abelian category (specialised to
23	`ModuleCat R`), as well as the regular-sequence definition
24	`RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`. The depth function `Module.depth`, the
25	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula, and the Cohen–Macaulay predicate are *not* in
26	Mathlib at the pinned commit — they are the new content scaffolded here.

74	- `depth` returns `ℕ∞` and is defined by the regular-sequence supremum.
75	- `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` is encoded as the depth-bound `↔` `Ext`

112	/-! ## §1. Depth of a finite module
113	
114	The `I`-depth of a finite `R`-module `M` is the supremum in
115	`{0, 1, 2, …, ∞}` of the lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in `I`
116	(provided `IM ≠ M`; if `IM = M` we set `depth_I(M) = ∞`). Mathlib `b80f227`
117	exposes the regular-sequence predicate `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`
118	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.RegularSequence`) but not the resulting
119	numeric depth function — that is the gap this declaration fills.
120	
121	Blueprint reference: `def:depth` (Stacks tag 00LF). -/
122	
123	/-- The **`I`-depth** of a finite `R`-module `M`: the supremum (in `ℕ∞`) of
124	lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in the ideal `I`.
125	
126	When `IM = M` (the "trivial-quotient" case, e.g. `M = 0` or `I = R`) the
127	supremum is taken to be `⊤` by convention. When `(R, 𝔪)` is local one usually
128	calls `depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M` simply *the depth* of `M`.
129	
130	iter-176+: the body is the supremum
131	```
132	sSup { (n : ℕ∞) | ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = n ∧ (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ I) ∧
133	                  RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs }
134	```
135	folded with the `IM = M` clause. The signature is non-tautological: it
136	asserts a function `(Ideal R) → (M : Type v) → ℕ∞` matching the Stacks
137	00LF definition.
138	
139	**iter-179 Mathlib-gap check (Lane F Target 2 STRETCH)**: a pinned-commit
140	audit of `Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.Depth` (the only file under
141	`Mathlib/RingTheory/*` containing the word "depth") confirms that Mathlib
142	`b80f227` ships only depth-zero lemmas (`IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff`)
143	and *not* the numeric depth function itself. The one-liner re-export route
144	is therefore not available; the body stays a typed `sorry` until an
145	iter-180+ body lane fills the supremum-with-`IM=M` clause directly. -/
146	noncomputable def depth {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (_I : Ideal R)
147	    (_M : Type v) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : ℕ∞ :=
148	  open Classical in
149	  if _I • (⊤ : Submodule R _M) = ⊤ then (⊤ : ℕ∞)
150	  else sSup { n : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = n ∧
151	    (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ _I) ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular _M rs }
152	
153	end Module
154	
155	end RingTheory

157	/-! ## §2. Projective dimension
158	
159	Mathlib `b80f227` exposes the categorical
160	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension : C → WithBot ℕ∞` on an abelian category
161	with enough projectives (file `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.Projective.Dimension`).
162	For `R`-modules this specialises to `ModuleCat.of R M`. The blueprint pins the
163	`Module.projectiveDimension` name as the re-export that downstream consumers
164	can use directly on an `R`-module without first packaging it in `ModuleCat`.
165	
166	Blueprint reference: `def:projective_dimension`. -/
167	
168	namespace Module
169	
170	/-- The **projective dimension** of an `R`-module `M`, defined as the
171	categorical projective dimension of `ModuleCat.of R M`.
172	
173	This is a re-export of `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` specialised to
174	the abelian category `ModuleCat R`. The categorical definition is the
175	infimum (in `WithBot ℕ∞`) of `n : ℕ` such that all `Ext^i(M, -)` vanish for
176	`i > n`, equivalently the smallest length of a projective resolution of `M`.
177	
178	Mathlib has the categorical `projectiveDimension` and the module-specific
179	`ModuleCat.projectiveDimension_eq_of_linearEquiv`; the blueprint pins the
180	wrapper name so downstream consumers can write
181	`Module.projectiveDimension R M` rather than threading `ModuleCat.of`.
182	
183	The body is the one-line re-export
184	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)`; iter-178 closed
185	this declaration kernel-clean. -/
186	noncomputable def projectiveDimension (R : Type u) [Ring R]
187	    (_M : Type u) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : WithBot ℕ∞ :=
188	  CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)
189	
190	end Module

196	/-! ## §3. Depth via Ext characterisation
197	
198	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a
199	nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the depth of `M` equals the smallest index `i`
200	at which `Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero (Stacks tag 00LP).
201	
202	This is the lemma A.4.a's downstream consumer ultimately reads off:
203	combined with the regular-sequence definition (`depth`), the Ext
204	characterisation provides both the *lower bound* (regular sequences exhibit
205	`Ext^i = 0` for `i < length(rs)`) and the *upper bound* (failure of any
206	extension lifts a non-zero element in `Ext^{depth}(κ, M)`).
207	
208	The signature pins the equivalence via the depth-bound `↔` `Ext`-vanishing-
209	below: `n ≤ depth(M) ↔ Ext^i(κ, M) = 0 for all i < n`. This is logically
210	equivalent to "depth(M) = smallest i with Ext^i ≠ 0" and is the form most
211	convenient for inductive proofs (Stacks 00LP proof: pick `x ∈ 𝔪`
212	non-zero-divisor, use long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` on `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0`).
213	
214	Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_via_ext` (Stacks tag 00LP). -/
215	
216	/-! ### Helper C (iter-183 Lane G, axiom-clean): `Ann`-killing of Ext via R-linearity
217	
218	For any `R`-modules `N, M` and any `x : R` in the annihilator of `N`, the
219	R-action `x • e` on `e : Ext^i_R(N, M)` is zero.
220	
221	Proof sketch: `x • e = (mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N)).comp e (zero_add i)` (by R-linearity:
222	`mk₀_smul + smul_comp + mk₀_id_comp`). For `x ∈ Ann(N)` the morphism
223	`x • 𝟙_N : N ⟶ N` is the zero map, so `mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N) = mk₀ 0 = 0`
224	(`mk₀_zero`), and `0.comp e = 0` (`zero_comp`).
225	
226	iter-183 Lane G: closed kernel-clean. This is the precise statement of the
227	Stacks-00LP "`x ∈ 𝔪` annihilates `Ext^*(κ, -)`" trick, lifted to the more
228	general `x ∈ Ann(N)` form so it covers both `N = κ` and `N = R/(x_1,…,x_k)`. -/
229	private lemma ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator
230	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
231	    {N M : ModuleCat.{u} R} {i : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} N M i)
232	    {x : R} (hx : x ∈ Module.annihilator R (N : Type u)) :
233	    x • e = 0 := by
234	  -- Step 1: x • 𝟙_N = 0 in ModuleCat (the underlying linear map sends m ↦ x • m,
235	  -- which is 0 since x ∈ Ann(N)).
236	  have hkill : (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N)) = (0 : N ⟶ N) := by
237	    apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
238	    apply LinearMap.ext
239	    intro n
240	    change x • n = 0
241	    exact Module.mem_annihilator.mp hx n
242	  -- Step 2: x • e = (mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N)).comp e (zero_add i) by R-linearity.
243	  have hreflect :
244	      (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N))).comp e (zero_add i)
245	        = x • e := by
246	    have hmk : (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N))
247	                : Abelian.Ext.{u} N N 0)
248	        = x • CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (𝟙 N) :=
249	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul (R := R) x (𝟙 N)
250	    rw [hmk, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_comp,
251	        CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp]
252	  -- Step 3: substitute hkill to collapse mk₀ … to mk₀ 0 = 0, then zero_comp.
253	  rw [← hreflect, hkill, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_zero,
254	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]
255	
256	/-- **Depth via Ext characterisation.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`
257	with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the
258	depth-bound `n ≤ depth(M)` is equivalent to the vanishing of `Ext^i_R(κ, M)`
259	for all `i < n`. Equivalently, `depth(M)` is the smallest `i` at which
260	`Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero.
261	
262	iter-176+: the body proceeds by induction on `n` via the long exact sequence
263	of `Ext^*(κ, -)` applied to `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0` for a non-zero-divisor
264	`x ∈ 𝔪`. The base case `n = 0` is `Hom(κ, M) ≠ 0 ↔ depth(M) = 0`, which is
265	the standard "the maximal ideal contains a zero-divisor on `M` iff
266	`𝔪 ∈ Ass(M)`" characterisation (Stacks 00LC).
267	
268	iter-183 Lane G structural progress:
269	* Generalised the inductive hypothesis over `M` (so `ih` is universally
270	  quantified in `M`, allowing recursive use on `M/xM`).
271	* Base case `n = 0` closed kernel-clean (both directions trivial).
272	* New axiom-clean helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` (lines
273	  229–254) discharges substantive piece (a) of the Stacks 00LP proof:
274	  *"`x ∈ Ann N` annihilates `Ext^*(N, ·)`"*, via
275	  `mk₀_smul + smul_comp + mk₀_id_comp + mk₀_zero + zero_comp`.
276	* The inductive step's **backward direction** has the regular-element
277	  extraction stage closed structurally: from the `Ext^0(κ, M) = 0`
278	  hypothesis we derive `Subsingleton (κ →ₗ[R] M)` (via `mk₀_eq_zero_iff +
279	  ModuleCat.hom_ext_iff`) and then invoke
280	  `IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff` + `Ideal.annihilator_quotient`
281	  to obtain `x ∈ 𝔪` with `IsSMulRegular M x`, with
282	  `nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal` providing the `Nontrivial
283	  (M/xM)` instance for the recursive IH application.
284	
285	Residual `sorry`s (2 named inline branches):
286	1. **Forward direction**  `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M → ∀ i ≤ n, Ext^i(κ, M) = 0` —
287	   needs: extract regular sequence of length n+1 from supremum (handle `⊤`
288	   via Nakayama); cons-decompose via `isRegular_cons_iff`; LES of Ext on
289	   `IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact`; recurse via `ih` on M/xM.
290	2. **Backward direction**, final assembly — after the regular-element
291	   extraction (already done), needs: LES chase to derive Ext-vanishing on
292	   M/xM at indices `j < n` (using the new helper to kill the
293	   multiplication-by-x maps); apply `ih (M := M/xM)` to obtain regular
294	   sequence `rs'`; cons `x :: rs'`; conclude via `le_sSup`. -/
295	theorem depth_eq_smallest_ext_index
296	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
297	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
298	    [Nontrivial M] (n : ℕ) :
299	    (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M ↔
300	      ∀ i : ℕ, i < n →
301	        ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
302	            (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
303	            (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 := by
304	  -- We generalize `M` so the inductive hypothesis is universally quantified
305	  -- over the module — this lets the induction step recursively apply the IH
306	  -- to the quotient `M / xM` (a *different* module of the same shape).
307	  induction n generalizing M with
308	  | zero =>
309	    -- LHS: `(0 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M` is `bot_le`.
310	    -- RHS: `∀ i < 0, …` is vacuous since no `i` satisfies `i < 0`.
311	    exact ⟨fun _ i hi _ => absurd hi (Nat.not_lt_zero i), fun _ => bot_le⟩
312	  | succ n ih =>
313	    -- The Stacks 00LP inductive step. The blueprint sketch is:
314	    --
315	    -- (⇒) Assume `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M`. Then `Nontrivial M` rules out
316	    --     `𝔪 • ⊤ = ⊤` (Nakayama), so `depth M` is the supremum and we can
317	    --     extract an `M`-regular sequence `rs = x :: rs'` of length `n+1` in
318	    --     `𝔪`. The cons-decomposition `RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff`
319	    --     gives `IsSMulRegular M x` and `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x M) rs'`.
320	    --     For `i = 0`: `Hom(κ, M) ↪ Hom(κ, M)` via `[x]` is `[x]` on the
321	    --     domain `Hom(κ, M)`, but `x ∈ 𝔪 = Ann(κ)` kills this on the κ side,
322	    --     so `Hom(κ, M) = 0`. Pass to `Ext^0` via `addEquiv₀`.
323	    --     For `1 ≤ i ≤ n`: the SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0` (built via
324	    --     `IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact`) plus the fact that
325	    --     `[x]_*` is zero on `Ext^i(κ, M)` (because `x ∈ Ann κ` ⇒
326	    --     `x • 𝟙_κ = 0`, hence by `precomp_smul = smul_precomp` the
327	    --     R-action on `Ext^i(κ, M)` is annihilated by `x`) lets the
328	    --     LES connecting map `Ext^{i-1}(κ, M/xM) ↠ Ext^i(κ, M)` be
329	    --     surjective.  By IH applied to `M/xM` (we get `n ≤ depth (M/xM)`,
330	    --     so `Ext^j(κ, M/xM) = 0` for `j < n`) we conclude
331	    --     `Ext^i(κ, M) = 0` for `1 ≤ i ≤ n`.
332	    --
333	    -- (⇐) Assume `∀ i < n+1, ∀ e ∈ Ext^i(κ, M), e = 0`.
334	    --     Specialise at `i = 0` and use `Ext.addEquiv₀` to extract
335	    --     `Subsingleton (κ →ₗ[R] M)`.  Apply
336	    --     `IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff` (Mathlib) with
337	    --     `N := ResidueField R` and `Module.annihilator R (ResidueField R) =
338	    --     maximalIdeal R` to obtain `x ∈ 𝔪` with `IsSMulRegular M x`.
339	    --     The SES + same "x annihilates Ext^*(κ, ·)" fact give
340	    --     `Ext^j(κ, M/xM) = 0` for `j < n` (via the LES at indices `j ≤ n-1`).
341	    --     `M/xM := QuotSMulTop x M` is `Nontrivial` by
342	    --     `nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal` and `Module.Finite`
343	    --     as a quotient.  Apply `ih` on `M/xM` at index `n` to get a
344	    --     regular sequence `rs'` of length `n` in `𝔪` on `M/xM`.  Then
345	    --     `x :: rs'` is a regular sequence of length `n+1` in `𝔪` on `M`
346	    --     by `RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff`. This gives
347	    --     `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M` via `le_sSup` on the depth supremum.
348	    --
349	    -- iter-183: the helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` above closes
350	    -- the substantive piece (a) "`x ∈ Ann N` annihilates `Ext^i(N, M)`"; this
351	    -- is the algebraic fact under both directions of the iff. The remaining
352	    -- pieces are LES-of-Ext bookkeeping + supremum-extraction.
353	    --
354	    -- The remaining substantive Mathlib gaps for closing this step are:
355	    --   (b) Extracting `Module.annihilator R (ResidueField R) = maximalIdeal R`
356	    --       (a one-liner via `Ideal.annihilator_quotient`).
357	    --   (c) The "sSup gives a witness of length ≥ n+1" reasoning, which
358	    --       in `ℕ∞` requires handling the `⊤` case (Nakayama rules it out
359	    --       under `Nontrivial M`).
360	    --   (d) Lifting `IsSMulRegular`-cons via `isRegular_cons_iff`.
361	    --
362	    -- We open the backward direction's regular-element-extraction step
363	    -- below to expose the API path for the next iter; the forward
364	    -- direction's regular-sequence-extraction + LES chase is the larger
365	    -- body remaining as `sorry`.
366	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
367	    · -- (⇒) Forward direction: `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M → ∀ i ≤ n, Ext^i(κ, M) = 0`.
368	      -- iter-184 Lane G: closed via Nakayama-driven `depth = sSup`
369	      -- extraction + cons-decomposition + LES chase using
370	      -- `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` + `covariant_sequence_exact₁`.
371	      intro _hdepth i _hi _e
372	      -- Step 1: unfold `depth M = sSup S_M` (Nakayama rules out `𝔪 • ⊤ = ⊤`).
373	      have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
374	        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
375	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
376	      have hdepth_M_unfold :
377	          depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
378	            = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
379	              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
380	              ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
381	        rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
382	      -- Step 2: extract a regular sequence rs of length > n in 𝔪 on M.
383	      have hlt : (n : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
384	        calc (n : ℕ∞)
385	            < ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast Nat.lt_succ_self n
386	          _ ≤ _ := _hdepth
387	      rw [hdepth_M_unfold] at hlt
388	      obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs, hrs_len, hrs_mem, hrs_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ := lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
389	      have hrs_len_gt : n < rs.length := by
390	        rw [← hrs_len] at hk_lt
391	        exact_mod_cast hk_lt
392	      -- Step 3: rs is non-empty (length > n ≥ 0); decompose rs = x :: rs_tail.
393	      rcases rs with _ | ⟨x, rs_tail⟩
394	      · -- impossible: empty list has length 0, not > n.
395	        simp at hrs_len_gt
396	      have hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := hrs_mem x List.mem_cons_self
397	      have htail_mem : ∀ r ∈ rs_tail, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
398	        hrs_mem r (List.mem_cons_of_mem _ hr)
399	      have hcons := (RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_tail).mp hrs_reg
400	      have hxReg : IsSMulRegular M x := hcons.1
401	      have hrs_tail_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x M) rs_tail :=
402	        hcons.2
403	      have htail_len_ge : n ≤ rs_tail.length := by
404	        have h1 : n < (x :: rs_tail).length := hrs_len_gt
405	        simp [List.length_cons] at h1
406	        omega
407	      -- Step 4: `x ∈ Ann(κ)` via `Ideal.annihilator_quotient`.
408	      have hannih : Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) =
409	          IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := Ideal.annihilator_quotient
410	      have hxAnnih : x ∈ Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) :=
411	        hannih ▸ hxMem
412	      -- Step 5: build MxM and show depth MxM ≥ n via the prefix rs_tail.take n.
413	      let MxM : Type u := QuotSMulTop x M
414	      haveI : Nontrivial MxM :=
415	        nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal M hxMem
416	      haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R MxM := inferInstance
417	      have hne_MxM : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R MxM) ≠ ⊤ :=
418	        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
419	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
420	      let rs_n := rs_tail.take n
421	      have hrs_n_len : rs_n.length = n := by
422	        change (rs_tail.take n).length = n
423	        rw [List.length_take]
424	        omega
425	      have hrs_n_mem : ∀ r ∈ rs_n, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
426	        htail_mem r (List.mem_of_mem_take hr)
427	      have hrs_n_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs_n := by
428	        change RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM (rs_tail.take n)
429	        have hsplit : rs_tail = rs_tail.take n ++ rs_tail.drop n :=
430	          (List.take_append_drop _ _).symm
431	        have hwr : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM rs_tail :=
432	          hrs_tail_reg.toIsWeaklyRegular
433	        rw [hsplit] at hwr
434	        have hwr_n : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM (rs_tail.take n) :=
435	          ((RingTheory.Sequence.isWeaklyRegular_append_iff MxM _ _).mp hwr).1
436	        exact (IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal
437	          hrs_n_mem).mpr hwr_n
438	      have hdepth_MxM : (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM := by
439	        rw [depth, if_neg hne_MxM]
440	        apply le_sSup
441	        refine ⟨rs_n, ?_, hrs_n_mem, hrs_n_reg⟩
442	        exact_mod_cast hrs_n_len
443	      -- Step 6: apply ih (M := MxM) at index n.
444	      have hMxM_vanish : ∀ j < n, ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
445	          (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
446	          (ModuleCat.of R MxM) j, e = 0 :=
447	        (ih (M := MxM)).mp hdepth_MxM
448	      -- Step 7: LES chase. Set up SES.
449	      let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
450	        ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
451	      have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
452	      -- `S.f = x • 𝟙_M` (definitional), hence `mk₀ S.f = x • mk₀ 𝟙`, hence
453	      -- `_e.comp (mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = x • _e = 0` (by helper at `x ∈ Ann κ`).
454	      have hSf_eq_smul : S.f = x • (𝟙 (ModuleCat.of R M) : _ ⟶ _) := rfl
455	      have hSf_kill :
456	          _e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = 0 := by
457	        have hcomp :
458	            _e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = x • _e := by
459	          rw [hSf_eq_smul]
460	          exact (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_eq_comp_mk₀ _e x).symm
461	        rw [hcomp]
462	        exact ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator _e hxAnnih
463	      -- Split on i = 0 vs i ≥ 1.
464	      rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i with hi0 | hi_pos
465	      · subst hi0
466	        -- Use mono S.f (since hxReg) + postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono.
467	        haveI hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f := by
468	          rw [ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
469	          exact hxReg
470	        have hinj := CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono
471	          (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)) S.f
472	        apply hinj
473	        change _e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero 0)
474	          = (0 : Abelian.Ext.{u} (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
475	              (ModuleCat.of R M) 0).comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f)
476	              (add_zero 0)
477	        rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]
478	        exact hSf_kill
479	      · -- i ≥ 1: write i = j + 1, then j < n and use covariant_sequence_exact₁.
480	        obtain ⟨j, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero
481	          (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hi_pos)
482	        have hj : j < n := by omega
483	        obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
484	          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁
485	            (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)) hS _e hSf_kill rfl
486	        have hx₃_zero : x₃ = 0 := hMxM_vanish j hj _
487	        rw [hx₃_zero] at hx₃
488	        simpa using hx₃.symm
489	    · -- (⇐) Backward direction: `(∀ i ≤ n, Ext^i(κ, M) = 0) → (n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M`.
490	      intro hext
491	      -- Step 1: From Ext^0(κ, M) = 0, extract `Subsingleton (κ →ₗ[R] M)`.
492	      -- For all R-linear maps `f g : κ →ₗ[R] M`, `mk₀ (ofHom f) = 0` in Ext^0
493	      -- (by `hext 0`), so via `mk₀_eq_zero_iff` the morphism `ofHom f = 0`,
494	      -- hence `f = 0`.  Both `f = 0 = g`.
495	      have hext0 : ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
496	          (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
497	          (ModuleCat.of R M) 0, e = 0 := hext 0 (Nat.succ_pos n)
498	      have hsubsing : Subsingleton (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R →ₗ[R] M) := by
499	        refine ⟨fun f g => ?_⟩
500	        have hf : (ModuleCat.ofHom f : ModuleCat.of R _ ⟶ ModuleCat.of R M) = 0 :=
501	          (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_eq_zero_iff (ModuleCat.ofHom f)).mp
502	            (hext0 _)
503	        have hg : (ModuleCat.ofHom g : ModuleCat.of R _ ⟶ ModuleCat.of R M) = 0 :=
504	          (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_eq_zero_iff (ModuleCat.ofHom g)).mp
505	            (hext0 _)
506	        have hf' : f = 0 := ModuleCat.hom_ext_iff.mp hf
507	        have hg' : g = 0 := ModuleCat.hom_ext_iff.mp hg
508	        rw [hf', hg']
509	      -- Step 2: Apply `subsingleton_linearMap_iff` to extract a regular element.
510	      -- The annihilator of `R ⧸ maximalIdeal R = ResidueField R` is `maximalIdeal R`
511	      -- by `Ideal.annihilator_quotient` (under the two-sided instance — automatic
512	      -- for commutative R).
513	      have hannih : Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) =
514	          IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R :=
515	        Ideal.annihilator_quotient
516	      have ⟨x, hxAnnih, hxReg⟩ :=
517	        IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff.mp hsubsing
518	      have hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := hannih ▸ hxAnnih
519	      -- Step 3: Pass to `M/xM := QuotSMulTop x M` and apply IH at index n.
520	      -- iter-184+: the remaining LES chase to derive Ext-vanishing on M/xM,
521	      -- application of IH, and `isRegular_cons_iff` assembly closes the rest.
522	      -- We package the partial state in named witnesses for the next iter.
523	      let MxM : Type u := QuotSMulTop x M
524	      haveI : Nontrivial MxM :=
525	        nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal M hxMem
526	      -- `Module.Finite R (M / xM)` is automatic via `Module.Finite.quotient`.
527	      haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R MxM := inferInstance
528	      -- iter-184 Lane G: closed via LES chase + sSup extraction + cons.
529	      --
530	      -- Step A: derive `∀ j < n, Ext^j(κ, MxM) = 0` from `hext` via the
531	      --   LES of `Ext^*(κ, ·)` on the SES `0 → M →[x] M → MxM → 0`.
532	      let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
533	        ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
534	      have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
535	      set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
536	        with hκ
537	      have hMxM_vanish : ∀ j < n, ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ
538	          (ModuleCat.of R MxM) j, e = 0 := by
539	        intro j hj e
540	        -- `e.comp hS.extClass rfl : Ext κ M (j+1) = 0` by `hext` at `j+1`.
541	        have he_ext : e.comp hS.extClass (rfl : j + 1 = j + 1) = 0 :=
542	          hext (j + 1) (by omega) _
543	        obtain ⟨x₂, hx₂⟩ :=
544	          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ κ hS e rfl he_ext
545	        -- `x₂ : Ext κ M j = 0` by `hext` at `j`.
546	        have hx₂_zero : x₂ = 0 := hext j (by omega) _
547	        rw [hx₂_zero] at hx₂
548	        simpa using hx₂.symm
549	      -- Step B: apply `ih (M := MxM)` at index `n`.
550	      have hdepth_MxM : (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM :=
551	        (ih (M := MxM)).mpr hMxM_vanish
552	      -- Step C: unfold `depth MxM = sSup`-clause and extract a witness rs'
553	      --   of length ≥ n on MxM in 𝔪 (when n ≥ 1; the n = 0 case uses []).
554	      have hne_MxM : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R MxM) ≠ ⊤ :=
555	        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
556	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
557	      have hdepth_MxM_unfold :
558	          depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM
559	            = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
560	              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
561	              ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs } := by
562	        rw [depth, if_neg hne_MxM]
563	      obtain ⟨rs', hrs'_len_ge, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩ :
564	          ∃ rs' : List R, n ≤ rs'.length ∧
565	            (∀ r ∈ rs', r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ∧
566	            RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs' := by
567	        rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with hn0 | hn_pos
568	        · subst hn0
569	          exact ⟨[], by simp, by simp,
570	            RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.nil R MxM⟩
571	        · have hlt : ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) <
572	              depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM := by
573	            have h1 : ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) < (n : ℕ∞) := by
574	              exact_mod_cast Nat.sub_lt hn_pos Nat.one_pos
575	            exact h1.trans_le hdepth_MxM
576	          rw [hdepth_MxM_unfold] at hlt
577	          obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs', hrs'_len_eq, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ :=
578	            lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
579	          have hrs'_len_gt : n - 1 < rs'.length := by
580	            rw [← hrs'_len_eq] at hk_lt
581	            exact_mod_cast hk_lt
582	          refine ⟨rs', ?_, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩
583	          omega
584	      -- Step D: truncate rs' to length n; resulting sequence is M-regular.
585	      let rs_n := rs'.take n
586	      have hrs_n_len : rs_n.length = n := by
587	        change (rs'.take n).length = n
588	        rw [List.length_take]
589	        omega
590	      have hrs_n_mem : ∀ r ∈ rs_n, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
591	        hrs'_mem r (List.mem_of_mem_take hr)
592	      have hrs_n_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs_n := by
593	        change RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM (rs'.take n)
594	        have hsplit : rs' = rs'.take n ++ rs'.drop n :=
595	          (List.take_append_drop _ _).symm
596	        have hwr : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM rs' :=
597	          hrs'_reg.toIsWeaklyRegular
598	        rw [hsplit] at hwr
599	        have hwr_n : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM (rs'.take n) :=
600	          ((RingTheory.Sequence.isWeaklyRegular_append_iff MxM _ _).mp hwr).1
601	        exact (IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal
602	          hrs_n_mem).mpr hwr_n
603	      -- Step E: cons x to form a length-(n+1) M-regular sequence in 𝔪.
604	      have hrs_M_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M (x :: rs_n) :=
605	        (RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_n).mpr ⟨hxReg, hrs_n_reg⟩
606	      have hrs_M_mem : ∀ r ∈ (x :: rs_n), r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
607	        intro r hr
608	        rcases List.mem_cons.mp hr with rfl | hr
609	        · exact hxMem
610	        · exact hrs_n_mem r hr
611	      have hrs_M_len : (x :: rs_n).length = n + 1 := by simp [hrs_n_len]
612	      -- Step F: conclude `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M` via `le_sSup`.
613	      have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
614	        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
615	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
616	      rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
617	      apply le_sSup
618	      refine ⟨x :: rs_n, ?_, hrs_M_mem, hrs_M_reg⟩
619	      exact_mod_cast hrs_M_len
620	
621	/-! ## §4. Depth on a short exact sequence
622	
623	For a short exact sequence `0 → N' → N → N'' → 0` of nonzero finite modules
624	over a Noetherian local ring, the three modules' depths satisfy three
625	crosswise inequalities (Stacks tag 00LE), each a direct read-off of the
626	long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` sequence and the depth-via-Ext characterisation
627	of §3.
628	
629	Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_short_exact_sequence` (Stacks tag 00LE). -/
630	
631	/-! ### Helper A (iter-182 Lane G, axiom-clean): Ext-vanishing from strict depth bound
632	
633	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`,
634	if `(i : ℕ∞) < depth M` then every element of `Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is zero.
635	
636	This packages `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` for the LES chase: the
637	`n ≤ depth M` form with `n := i + 1` instantiates the `∀ j < i + 1`
638	quantifier at `j = i`. Body is kernel-clean modulo the typed sorry of
639	`depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`. -/
640	private lemma ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth
641	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
642	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
643	    [Nontrivial M] {i : ℕ}
644	    (h : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M)
645	    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.{u}
646	        (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
647	        (ModuleCat.of R M) i) : e = 0 := by
648	  have h' : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
649	    have hcast : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (i : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
650	    rw [hcast]; exact Order.add_one_le_of_lt h
651	  exact (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (i + 1)).mp h' i (Nat.lt_succ_self i) e
652	
653	/-! ### Helper B (iter-182 Lane G, axiom-clean): `ℕ∞` tsub bridge
654	
655	If `(a : ℕ) ≤ d - 1` in `ℕ∞` and `1 ≤ a` (in `ℕ`), then
656	`((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ d`.
657	
658	Case-split on `d = ⊤` (trivial) and `d = ↑n` (drop to `ℕ` arithmetic).
659	Used for the `depth N' - 1` shift in the second SES inequality. -/
660	private lemma natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one
661	    {d : ℕ∞} {a : ℕ} (ha : 1 ≤ a) (h : (a : ℕ∞) ≤ d - 1) :
662	    ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ d := by
663	  rcases eq_or_ne d ⊤ with hd | hd
664	  · simp [hd]
665	  · obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := WithTop.ne_top_iff_exists.mp hd
666	    -- Reduce to ℕ: turn `↑a ≤ ↑n - 1` into `a ≤ n - 1`, then `a + 1 ≤ n`.
667	    have h₂ : (a : ℕ∞) ≤ ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by
668	      refine h.trans (le_of_eq ?_)
669	      rcases n with _ | n
670	      · rfl
671	      · push_cast; rfl
672	    have han : a ≤ n - 1 := by exact_mod_cast h₂
673	    have hle : a + 1 ≤ n := by omega
674	    exact Nat.cast_le.mpr hle
675	
676	theorem depth_of_short_exact
677	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
678	    {N' N N'' : Type u}
679	    [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] [_root_.Module.Finite R N'] [Nontrivial N']
680	    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [_root_.Module.Finite R N] [Nontrivial N]
681	    [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N''] [_root_.Module.Finite R N''] [Nontrivial N'']
682	    (f : N' →ₗ[R] N) (g : N →ₗ[R] N'')
683	    (_hf : Function.Injective f) (_hg : Function.Surjective g)
684	    (_hex : Function.Exact f g) :
685	    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N')
686	        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'')
687	      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N
688	    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
689	          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1)
690	        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N''
691	    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
692	          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1)
693	        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
694	  -- Package the SES as a `ShortComplex.ShortExact` in `ModuleCat.{u} R`.
695	  let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
696	    ShortComplex.mk (ModuleCat.ofHom f) (ModuleCat.ofHom g)
697	      (by ext x; simpa using _hex.apply_apply_eq_zero x)
698	  have hS : S.ShortExact :=
699	    ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact S _hex _hf _hg
700	  -- The residue field as a ModuleCat object.
701	  set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) with hκ
702	  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
703	  · -- (1) min(depth N', depth N'') ≤ depth N
704	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
705	    intro a ha
706	    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
707	    obtain ⟨haN', haN''⟩ := ha
708	    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
709	    intro i hi e
710	    -- `e : Ext κ S.X₂ i = Ext κ (of R N) i`; goal `e = 0`.
711	    have hicast : (i : ℕ∞) < (a : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hi
712	    have hiN' : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := hicast.trans_le haN'
713	    have hiN'' : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' := hicast.trans_le haN''
714	    -- `e ∘ S.g ∈ Ext κ (of R N'') i = 0`.
715	    have heg : e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero i) = 0 :=
716	      ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN'' _
717	    obtain ⟨x₁, hx₁⟩ :=
718	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂ κ hS e heg
719	    -- `x₁ ∈ Ext κ (of R N') i = 0`.
720	    have hx₁_zero : x₁ = 0 := ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN' _
721	    rw [hx₁_zero] at hx₁
722	    simpa using hx₁.symm
723	  · -- (2) min(depth N, depth N' - 1) ≤ depth N''
724	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
725	    intro a ha
726	    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
727	    obtain ⟨haN, haN'sub⟩ := ha
728	    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
729	    intro i hi e
730	    -- `e : Ext κ S.X₃ i = Ext κ (of R N'') i`; goal `e = 0`.
731	    have hicast : (i : ℕ∞) < (a : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hi
732	    have hiN : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N := hicast.trans_le haN
733	    -- `↑(i+1) < depth N'`: use Helper B with `a` and the inequality `hi : i + 1 ≤ a`.
734	    have hia : 1 ≤ a := by omega
735	    have ha1 : ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' :=
736	      natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one hia haN'sub
737	    have hsucc : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
738	      have : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) < ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast Nat.add_lt_add_right hi 1
739	      exact this.trans_le ha1
740	    -- `e ∘ extClass ∈ Ext κ (of R N') (i + 1) = 0`.
741	    have hext : e.comp hS.extClass rfl = 0 :=
742	      ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hsucc _
743	    obtain ⟨x₂, hx₂⟩ :=
744	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ κ hS e rfl hext
745	    -- `x₂ ∈ Ext κ (of R N) i = 0`.
746	    have hx₂_zero : x₂ = 0 := ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN _
747	    rw [hx₂_zero] at hx₂
748	    simpa using hx₂.symm
749	  · -- (3) min(depth N, depth N'' + 1) ≤ depth N'
750	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
751	    intro a ha
752	    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
753	    obtain ⟨haN, haN''add⟩ := ha
754	    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
755	    intro i hi e
756	    -- `e : Ext κ S.X₁ i = Ext κ (of R N') i`; goal `e = 0`.
757	    have hicast : (i : ℕ∞) < (a : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hi
758	    have hiN : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N := hicast.trans_le haN
759	    -- `e ∘ S.f ∈ Ext κ (of R N) i = 0`.
760	    have hef : e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = 0 :=
761	      ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN _
762	    -- Split on `i = 0` vs `i ≥ 1`. For `i ≥ 1`, use `covariant_sequence_exact₁`.
763	    -- For `i = 0`, postcomposition by `S.f` is injective (since `S.f` is mono).
764	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i with hi0 | hi0
765	    · subst hi0
766	      -- `e : Ext κ S.X₁ 0`; postcomp by `S.f` is injective; image is `e ∘ S.f = 0`,
767	      -- so `e = 0`.
768	      have hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f :=
769	        (ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective _).mpr _hf
770	      have hinj := CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono κ S.f
771	      apply hinj
772	      simpa using hef
773	    · -- `i ≥ 1`. Let `i = j + 1` and use `covariant_sequence_exact₁` at
774	      -- `n₀ = j, n₁ = i = j + 1`.
775	      obtain ⟨j, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hi0)
776	      -- `e : Ext κ (of R N') (j + 1)`. We need `Ext κ (of R N'') j = 0`.
777	      -- From `↑(j+2) ≤ ↑a ≤ depth N'' + 1`, get `↑j + 1 ≤ depth N''`, so `↑j < depth N''`.
778	      have hjN'' : (j : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' := by
779	        have hja : j + 2 ≤ a := by omega
780	        have h_j2 : ((j + 2 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1 := by
781	          refine le_trans ?_ haN''add
782	          exact_mod_cast hja
783	        have hcast : ((j + 2 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = ((j + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
784	        rw [hcast] at h_j2
785	        have h_canc : ((j + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' :=
786	          (ENat.add_le_add_iff_right (by norm_num : (1 : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤)).mp h_j2
787	        have hcast2 : ((j + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (j : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
788	        rw [hcast2] at h_canc
789	        exact (ENat.add_one_le_iff (by simp : (j : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤)).mp h_canc
790	      obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
791	        CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ κ hS e hef rfl
792	      -- `x₃ ∈ Ext κ (of R N'') j = 0`.
793	      have hx₃_zero : x₃ = 0 := ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hjN'' _
794	      rw [hx₃_zero] at hx₃
795	      simpa using hx₃.symm
