797	/-! ### Helper iter-193 Lane G (axiom-clean): depth is preserved under R-linear equivalence
798	
799	For a commutative ring `R`, an ideal `I ⊆ R`, and two `R`-modules `M, M'` with
800	an `R`-linear equivalence `e : M ≃ₗ[R] M'`, we have `depth I M = depth I M'`.
801	
802	This is the standard "depth is an invariant of the isomorphism class" fact;
803	the proof has two steps: (1) the side-condition `I • ⊤ = ⊤` is preserved
804	under linear equivalence, and (2) the regular-sequence supremum sets agree
805	via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`. Both halves are direct Mathlib calls; the
806	helper is axiom-clean.
807	
808	iter-193 Lane G structural advance: this helper is the substrate piece
809	needed to identify `depth(M)` with `depth(R^k)` for `M` finite free, which
810	in turn closes the `pd(M) = 0` base case of the Auslander–Buchsbaum
811	formula modulo the `depth(R^k) = depth(R)` direct-sum step (a separate
812	substrate item; the file's residual sorries are narrowed to that one
813	named piece). -/
814	lemma depth_eq_of_linearEquiv {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R)
815	    {M M' : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R M']
816	    (e : M ≃ₗ[R] M') :
817	    depth I M = depth I M' := by
818	  -- Step 1: `I • ⊤ = ⊤` is preserved under the linear equivalence.
819	  have hcond : (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤) ↔ (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M') = ⊤) := by
820	    have e_top : Submodule.map (e : M →ₗ[R] M') (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤ := by
821	      rw [Submodule.map_top]; exact LinearEquiv.range e
822	    have e_symm_top :
823	        Submodule.map (e.symm : M' →ₗ[R] M) (⊤ : Submodule R M') = ⊤ := by
824	      rw [Submodule.map_top]; exact LinearEquiv.range e.symm
825	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
826	    · intro h
827	      have hmap :=
828	        Submodule.map_smul'' I (⊤ : Submodule R M) (e : M →ₗ[R] M')
829	      rw [h, e_top] at hmap
830	      exact hmap.symm
831	    · intro h
832	      have hmap :=
833	        Submodule.map_smul'' I (⊤ : Submodule R M') (e.symm : M' →ₗ[R] M)
834	      rw [h, e_symm_top] at hmap
835	      exact hmap.symm
836	  -- Step 2: the `sSup` sets agree via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`.
837	  unfold depth
838	  by_cases h : I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤
839	  · simp [if_pos h, if_pos (hcond.mp h)]
840	  · rw [if_neg h, if_neg (mt hcond.mpr h)]
841	    congr 1
842	    ext n
843	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
844	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
845	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e rs).mp hreg⟩
846	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
847	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e rs).mpr hreg⟩
848	
849	/-! ### Helper iter-194 Lane G (axiom-clean): depth of constant Pi equals depth of fiber.
850	
851	For a commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`, module `M`, and nonempty finite type `ι`,
852	`depth I (ι → M) = depth I M`. The proof goes through the regular-sequence
853	characterization: each `r`-action on `ι → M` is pointwise (so an `r ∈ R` is
854	regular on `ι → M` iff regular on `M`), and the quotient `(ι → M)/r·⊤`
855	identifies with `ι → M/r·⊤` via `Submodule.quotientPi`. The side condition
856	`I • ⊤ = ⊤` agrees on both sides via a `Pi.single` lifting argument.
857	
858	This is the substrate needed to close the `pd_R(M) = 0` case of the
859	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula (where `M ≃ₗ[R] Fin k → R` via a basis). -/
860	
861	/-- For any commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`, finite index `ι`, and module `M`,
862	the ideal-action `I • ⊤_{ι → M}` equals the pi-submodule of fibre `I • ⊤_M`s. -/
863	private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const
864	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι]
865	    (I : Ideal R) {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
866	    (I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M))) =
867	      Submodule.pi (Set.univ : Set ι) (fun (_ : ι) => I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)) := by
868	  apply le_antisymm
869	  · intro f hf i _
870	    refine Submodule.smul_induction_on hf ?_ ?_
871	    · intro a hain x _
872	      change a • x i ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)
873	      exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul hain trivial
874	    · intro x y hx hy
875	      change (x + y) i ∈ _
876	      exact Submodule.add_mem _ hx hy
877	  · intro f hf
878	    rw [show f = ∑ j, Pi.single j (f j) from (Finset.univ_sum_single f).symm]
879	    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
880	    intro j _
881	    have hfj : f j ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) := hf j (Set.mem_univ j)
882	    have hmap :
883	        Pi.single j (f j) ∈
884	          Submodule.map (LinearMap.single R (fun (_ : ι) => M) j)
885	            (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)) :=
886	      Submodule.mem_map.mpr ⟨f j, hfj, rfl⟩
887	    rw [Submodule.map_smul''] at hmap
888	    exact Submodule.smul_mono le_rfl le_top hmap
889	
890	/-- The side condition `I • ⊤ = ⊤` agrees on `ι → M` and `M` for nonempty
891	finite `ι`: a free product of fibre `I•⊤_M`-witnesses combines to a
892	`I•⊤_{ι → M}`-witness (via `Pi.single`-lifting), and conversely a
893	`Pi.single j m`-projection at `j` reads off the witness on the fibre. -/
894	private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff
895	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
896	    (I : Ideal R) {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
897	    I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) = ⊤ ↔ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤ := by
898	  constructor
899	  · intro h
900	    rw [eq_top_iff]
901	    intro m _
902	    obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
903	    have hsingle_mem :
904	        (Pi.single j m : ι → M) ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) := by
905	      rw [h]; trivial
906	    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const] at hsingle_mem
907	    have := hsingle_mem j (Set.mem_univ j)
908	    rwa [Pi.single_eq_same] at this
909	  · intro h
910	    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const, eq_top_iff]
911	    intro f _ i _
912	    rw [h]
913	    trivial
914	
915	/-- `QuotSMulTop r (ι → M) ≃ₗ[R] ι → QuotSMulTop r M` for finite `ι`,
916	obtained by rewriting `r • ⊤ = Ideal.span {r} • ⊤` and using
917	`Submodule.quotientPi`. -/
918	private noncomputable def quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv
919	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (r : R)
920	    {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
921	    QuotSMulTop r (ι → M) ≃ₗ[R] (ι → QuotSMulTop r M) := by
922	  refine (Submodule.quotEquivOfEq _ _ ?_).trans (Submodule.quotientPi _)
923	  rw [← Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul r (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M))]
924	  rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const]
925	  congr 1
926	  funext _
927	  exact Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul r ⊤
928

930	is `M`-regular. Proof by induction on `rs`: the empty case reduces to

11	/-!
12	# Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)
13	
14	This file is the **A.4.a** sub-build chapter for the positive-genus arm of the
15	project's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the two extension inputs to
16	Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3 Theorem 3.2, plus the Weil-divisor
17	reformulation of the codim-`1` obstruction:
18	
19	* **Milne Theorem 3.1** (`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`): a rational map
20	  from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has indeterminacy locus
21	  of codimension `≥ 2`; if additionally the map is already
22	  codim-`1`-indeterminacy-free, then it extends to a regular morphism.
23	* **Milne Lemma 3.3** (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): for a
24	  rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
25	  indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension `1`.
26	* **Weil-divisor obstruction** (`mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`):
27	  `f` is defined at the generic point of a prime divisor `W` iff some
28	  `PartialMap` representative of `f` is regular at `W.point`. This is a thin
29	  re-shuffle of Mathlib's `RationalMap.mem_domain`; the substantive
30	  Hartshorne-II.6 "regular ⇔ order ≥ 0 at every DVR pullback" reformulation
31	  (the original Weil-divisor obstruction) is deferred to a future lemma
32	  that builds genuine pullback machinery (`Scheme.RationalMap.order`-along-`f`),
33	  outside this iter's scope.
34	
35	These outputs feed Milne Theorem 3.2 in the sibling
36	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` (A.4.c) and consume
37	`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b)
38	at Step 2 of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`.
39	
40	## Status (iter-177 Lane 6 file-skeleton)
41	
42	Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended*
43	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
44	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
45	Bodies are iter-178+ work, gated on the local-cohomology / depth-≥2
46	extension lemma (Step 2 of `thm:codim_one_extension`) being supplied either
47	from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (the `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`
48	input) or from a Mathlib upstream once the local-cohomology vanishing
49	`H¹_{x}(V, 𝒪_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point lands.
50	
51	The 6 pinned declarations are:
52	
53	1. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus` (def, ~3 LOC) — the closed
54	   complement of `RationalMap.domain` as a `Set X`.
55	2. `Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree` (def, ~3 LOC) — predicate: every
56	   point `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in `f.domain`.
57	3. `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (theorem, ~3 LOC) — for a smooth
58	   integral variety, the stalk at a codim-1 point is a DVR.
59	4. `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (theorem, ~10 LOC) —
60	   Milne 3.1 specialised to smooth source + complete target +
61	   codim-1-indeterminacy-free.
62	5. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
63	   (theorem, ~8 LOC) — Milne Lemma 3.3.
64	6. `Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`
65	   (theorem, ~2 LOC) — definitional re-shuffle of `RationalMap.mem_domain`
66	   exposing a `PartialMap` representative whose domain contains `W.point`.
67	   The substantive Weil-divisor `order ≥ 0` reformulation is deferred (see
68	   header).
69	
70	## Note on type expressivity
71	
72	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
73	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
74	
75	* `indeterminacyLocus f : Set X` returns the carrier of the closed
76	  complement of `f.domain` — not `∅` or `Set.univ`.
77	* `CodimOneFree f : Prop` is the genuine ∀-statement over codim-1 points,
78	  not `True`.
79	* `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` produces a Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing`
80	  instance — not a trivial proposition.
81	* `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` asserts `∃! g, g.toRationalMap = f`
82	  — the existence of a unique regular extension.
83	* `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` asserts a disjunction:
84	  either the locus is empty or every component has coheight `1`.
85	* `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` asserts the (truthful)
86	  iff between definedness at `W.point` and the existence of a `PartialMap`
87	  representative containing `W.point` in its domain — a definitional unfold
88	  of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` with the
89	  conjunction swapped for downstream ergonomics.
90	
91	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
92	(the carrier set complement, the `∀ η, ...` statement, the DVR instance,
93	the unique regular extension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content
94	`proof_wanted` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
95	
96	## Variety conventions
97	
98	Following the project (cf. `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`,
99	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), a **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** is an
100	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
101	
102	* `[Smooth X.hom]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
103	* `[IsSeparated X.hom]`, `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
104	* `[IsIntegral X.left]`, `[IsReduced X.left]`.
105	
106	A **complete variety** adds `[IsProper Y.hom]`. A **group variety** over
107	`k̄` adds a `[GrpObj Y]` instance to the variety package.
108	
109	## References
110

112	6 pins). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.1 (p. 16) and
113	Lemma 3.3 (p. 17); Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II.6 pp. 130–131.
114	-/

128	/-! ## §1. The indeterminacy locus of a rational map
129	
130	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed
131	complement of its domain of definition `Dom(f) := f.domain` (a Mathlib-shipped
132	open subset of `X`).
133	
134	Blueprint pin: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne §I.3 p. 16). -/
135	
136	/-- **Indeterminacy locus of a rational map.** The closed complement of the
137	domain of definition `f.domain` (an `X.Opens`, exposed by Mathlib at
138	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`):
139	
140	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.
141	
142	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff
143	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
144	
145	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
146	§I.3, p. 16). -/
147	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
148	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
149	
150	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
151	of an open). -/
152	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
153	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
154	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
155	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
156	
157	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
158	
159	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
160	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
161	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
162	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
163	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
164	`f.domain`.
165	
166	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
167	conclusion). -/
168	
169	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
170	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
171	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
172	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
173	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
174	
175	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
176	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
177	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
178	
179	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
180	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
181	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
182	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
183	
184	end RationalMap
185	
186	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
187	
188	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
189	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
190	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
191	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
192	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
193	
194	Blueprint pin: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130). -/
195	
196	/-! ### Stacks 00TT scaffolding (Lane M↓ iter-191 first scaffold)
197	
198	The Mathlib gap "smooth over `\bar k` ⟹ stalk is a regular local ring"
199	(Stacks tag `00TT`) is structured here as a four-stage pipeline:
200	
201	* **Stage 1** — smooth ⟹ flat at every stalk (Mathlib instance
202	  `AlgebraicGeometry.instFlatOfSmooth` + `Flat.stalkMap`).
203	* **Stage 2** — smooth at a point ⟹ exists a standard smooth presentation
204	  on an affine neighbourhood (Stacks tag `00T7`, packaged in Mathlib at
205	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`).
206	* **Stage 3** — the polynomial generators of the standard smooth
207	  presentation form a regular sequence after localising at any prime

583	    cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
584	  -- Promote to κ-linear via the residue surjection.
585	  have hSurj : Function.Surjective

632	/-! ### Stage 6 sub-gap (ii.B) Stacks-00OE substrate (iter-200)
633	
634	The residual Mathlib gap for Stage 6 is the smooth-algebra Krull-dim formula:
635	for a standard-smooth `k`-algebra `S` of relative dim `n` and a maximal ideal
636	`m ⊂ S` corresponding to a closed point over an algebraically closed base,
637	`ringKrullDim (Localization.AtPrime S m) = n`.
638	
639	The iter-200 `mathlib-analogist coe-stacks00oe` recipe ranks this as a 3-step
640	chain composing existing Mathlib pieces:
641	
642	* **Step 1** (`IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`, trivial):
643	  rewrite `ringKrullDim (Localization.AtPrime S m) = m.height`.
644	* **Step 2** (polynomial-ring height): for the standard-smooth presentation
645	  `MvPolynomial ι k ↠ S` with kernel a regular sequence, the contracted
646	  maximal ideal `m' ⊂ MvPolynomial ι k` has height `#ι`. This is built
647	  axiom-clean below via induction on `Fin n` along Mathlib's
648	  `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one` + `MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv`
649	  + `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing`.
650	* **Step 3** (regular-sequence drop, Stacks 00SW / 00OW): the relations of the
651	  standard-smooth presentation form a regular sequence in the localization,
652	  consumed via `ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`. The
653	  Jacobian-criterion regular-sequence witness is the substantive residual
654	  obligation; landing it requires `Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.jacobian_isUnit`
655	  + `IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal`
656	  scaffolding which is iter-201+ Lane COE substrate.
657	
658	Iter-200 lands Step 2 fully axiom-clean (`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card`
659	+ general-finite-ι transport `MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard`),
660	the Step 1 named bridge (`ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime`), and
661	the Step 3 quotient-form re-export
662	(`ringKrullDim_quotient_isRegular_eq_sub_length`) so the iter-201+ closure
663	collapses to building the Jacobian-regular-sequence witness alone. -/
664	
665	/-- **Step 1 bridge (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200).** Ergonomic re-export of
666	`IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`: for a prime ideal `m ⊂ R` and
667	a localization `A = R_m`, `ringKrullDim A = m.height`. This is the boundary
668	translator inside `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` between
669	`Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` (the iter-183 CoheightBridge bridge,
670	phrased in `ringKrullDim`) and the Mathlib polynomial-ring height arithmetic
671	(phrased in `Ideal.height`).
672	
673	Axiom-clean: 1-line re-export. -/
674	private theorem ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime
675	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (m : Ideal R) [m.IsPrime]
676	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] :
677	    ringKrullDim A = m.height :=
678	  IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m A
679	
680	/-- **Step 2 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), `Fin n` form.** For a
681	field `k` and a maximal ideal `m ⊂ MvPolynomial (Fin n) k`, the height of
682	`m` equals `n`.
683	
684	This is the substantive Step 2 ingredient: combined with the standard-smooth
685	presentation surjection `MvPolynomial (Fin (#ι)) k ↠ S`, it computes the
686	height of any contracted maximal ideal `m' = preimage(m) ⊂ MvPolynomial (Fin (#ι)) k`
687	explicitly. The closed-point case is the substantive one — every maximal ideal
688	of `MvPolynomial (Fin n) k` has height exactly `n` (independent of whether `k`
689	is algebraically closed; the Jacobson property suffices).
690	
691	Axiom-clean proof: induction on `n`. The base case `n = 0` is trivial (no
692	maximal-ideal content beyond `bot_le`). The inductive step transports `m` along
693	`MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv` to a maximal ideal `m'` of
694	`Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)`, contracts to `p = m' ∩ MvPolynomial (Fin n) k`
695	(maximal by `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` since
696	`MvPolynomial (Fin n) k` is Jacobson), and applies `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`
697	followed by the inductive hypothesis on `p`. The upper bound follows from
698	`Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top` + `MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing`. -/
699	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_ge_card_of_field
700	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (n : ℕ) :
701	    ∀ (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) [m.IsMaximal], (n : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height := by
702	  induction n with
703	  | zero => intro m _; exact_mod_cast bot_le
704	  | succ n ih =>
705	    intro m _
706	    let e := MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv k n
707	    let m' : Ideal (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) := Ideal.map e.toRingEquiv m
708	    haveI : m'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv (e := e.toRingEquiv)
709	    let p : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k) := Ideal.comap Polynomial.C m'
710	    haveI : p.IsMaximal := Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing m'
711	    haveI : m'.LiesOver p := ⟨rfl⟩
712	    have ih' := ih p
713	    have hh : m'.height = p.height + 1 := Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one p m'
714	    have hm_eq : m.height = m'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map e.toRingEquiv m).symm
715	    rw [hm_eq, hh, show ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (n : ℕ∞) + 1 from by push_cast; ring]
716	    gcongr
717	
718	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_le_natCard_of_field.{v}
719	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {ιx : Type v} [Finite ιx]
720	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) (hm : m ≠ ⊤) :
721	    m.height ≤ Nat.card ιx := by
722	  have h1 : (m.height : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim (MvPolynomial ιx k) :=
723	    Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top hm
724	  rw [MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing, ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_field,
725	    zero_add] at h1
726	  exact_mod_cast h1
727	
728	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card
729	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (n : ℕ)
730	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) [hmax : m.IsMaximal] :
731	    m.height = n := by
732	  apply le_antisymm
733	  · have := MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_le_natCard_of_field k (ιx := Fin n) m hmax.ne_top
734	    simpa using this
735	  · exact MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_ge_card_of_field k n m
736	
737	/-- **Step 2 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), general `[Finite ι]` form.**
738	For a field `k`, a finite index type `ι`, and a maximal ideal
739	`m ⊂ MvPolynomial ι k`, the height of `m` equals `Nat.card ι`.
740	
741	Axiom-clean: transports the `Fin n` form
742	`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card` along the canonical
743	`MvPolynomial.renameEquiv` induced by `(Finite.equivFin ι)`. -/
744	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard.{v}
745	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (ιx : Type v) [Finite ιx]
746	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) [m.IsMaximal] :
747	    m.height = Nat.card ιx := by
748	  classical
749	  haveI : Fintype ιx := Fintype.ofFinite ιx
750	  let σ : ιx ≃ Fin (Fintype.card ιx) := Fintype.equivFin ιx
751	  let e : MvPolynomial ιx k ≃+* MvPolynomial (Fin (Fintype.card ιx)) k :=
752	    (MvPolynomial.renameEquiv k σ).toRingEquiv
753	  let m' : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin (Fintype.card ιx)) k) := Ideal.map e m
754	  haveI : m'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv (e := e)
755	  have hm_eq : m.height = m'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map e m).symm
756	  have hcard : Nat.card ιx = Fintype.card ιx := by simp [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card]
757	  rw [hm_eq, hcard]
758	  exact MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card k (Fintype.card ιx) m'
759	
760	/-- **Step 1 + Step 2 capstone (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200).** For any
761	localisation `A` of `MvPolynomial ιx k` at a maximal ideal `m`, the Krull
762	dimension of `A` equals `Nat.card ιx`.
763	
764	This is the most direct project-side consumer route for Step 1 + Step 2: it
765	takes the polynomial-ring height equality (`m.height = Nat.card ιx` via
766	`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard`) and pre-composes with the
767	Step 1 bridge (`ringKrullDim A = m.height` via
768	`ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime`) to expose the closed form
769	`ringKrullDim A = Nat.card ιx` without going through `Ideal.height`.
770	
771	Combined with Step 3 (`ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_of_regular_sequence`)
772	plus the Jacobian-regular-sequence witness (iter-201+ Lane COE), this yields
773	`ringKrullDim Sₘ = Nat.card ιx − rs.length` for the standard-smooth quotient
774	`Sₘ = A ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs`. -/
775	private theorem ringKrullDim_localization_atMaximal_MvPolynomial.{v}
776	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {ιx : Type v} [Finite ιx]
777	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) [m.IsMaximal]
778	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra (MvPolynomial ιx k) A]
779	    [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] :
780	    ringKrullDim A = (Nat.card ιx : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
781	  rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m A,
782	    MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard k ιx m]
783	  rfl
784	
785	/-- **Step 3 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), parameterised additive form.**
786	For a Noetherian local ring `R'` with `ringKrullDim R' = a` (a natural number) and
787	a regular sequence `rs : List R'` of length `b`, the Krull dimension of the
788	quotient `R' ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs` plus `b` equals `a`.
789	
790	This is the parameterised version of Mathlib's
791	`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular` packaged in `WithBot ℕ∞`
792	arithmetic, with the natural-number values of dimension and length explicit. It is
793	the additive form preferred over a subtraction form because `WithBot ℕ∞` does not
794	carry a subtraction structure.
795	
796	The consumer of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` chains:
797	
798	* `R'` = the polynomial-ring localisation `R'_{m'} = (MvPolynomial ι k)_{m'}`,
799	* `hDim` (a = #ι) = `MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard` composed with
800	  the Step 1 bridge `ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime`,
801	* `hReg` = the Jacobian-criterion regular-sequence witness (residual Mathlib gap;
802	  iter-201+ Lane COE),
803	* `hLen` (b = #σ) = the cardinality of the regular sequence.
804	
805	Then `ringKrullDim Sₘ = a − b = n` follows by cancellation from this lemma.
806	
807	Axiom-clean: 3-line re-export. -/
808	private theorem ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_of_regular_sequence
809	    {R' : Type*} [CommRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R'] [IsLocalRing R']
810	    (rs : List R') (hReg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R' rs)
811	    {a b : ℕ} (hDim : ringKrullDim R' = (a : WithBot ℕ∞))
812	    (hLen : rs.length = b) :
813	    ringKrullDim (R' ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (b : WithBot ℕ∞) = (a : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
814	  have h := ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular rs hReg
815	  rw [hDim, hLen] at h
816	  exact h

818	/-! ### Iter-201 Step A2 substrate (cotangent linear-independence transport)
819	
820	Two axiom-clean substrate helpers needed by the iter-201+ A2 step of the

831	  — the same family transported through the canonical localisation map

834	  helper with Mathlib's `LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule`.

844	/-- **Iter-201 Step A2 substrate, Mathlib gap-free part 1.** For a submersive
845	presentation `P : R → S` of an `R`-algebra, the family
846	`i ↦ Cotangent.mk ⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩`

849	This is a forward-ergonomics re-package of `P.basisCotangent.linearIndependent`
850	+ `P.basisCotangent_apply`: the basis-form lin-indep statement is restated
851	with the explicit `Cotangent.mk`-of-relation form on the consumer side, so
852	downstream callers do not need to manually unfold the basis abstraction.

855	private theorem submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent
856	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
857	    {ix sx : Type*} [Finite sx]
858	    (P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ix sx) :
859	    LinearIndependent S
860	      (fun i : sx => Algebra.Extension.Cotangent.mk
861	        (⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩ : P.toExtension.ker)) := by
862	  have h := P.basisCotangent.linearIndependent
863	  have heq : (fun i : sx => Algebra.Extension.Cotangent.mk
864	        (⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩ : P.toExtension.ker)) = P.basisCotangent := by
865	    funext i
866	    exact (P.basisCotangent_apply i).symm
867	  rw [heq]
868	  exact h

872	canonical localisation map of `P.toExtension.Cotangent` at any prime `p` of

874	is `Localization.AtPrime p`-linearly independent.

876	Composes the previous helper with Mathlib's
877	`LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule` (in
878	`Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Module`) and the canonical
879	`LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap` + `LocalizedModule.isModule`-driven

886	not landed here.

888	Axiom-clean: 1 invocation of `LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule` on top
889	of `submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent`. -/
890	private theorem submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent_localized
891	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
892	    {ix sx : Type*} [Finite sx]
893	    (P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ix sx)
894	    (p : Ideal S) [p.IsPrime] :
895	    LinearIndependent (Localization.AtPrime p)
896	      (fun i : sx =>
897	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent)

900	  LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule (Localization.AtPrime p) p.primeCompl

902	    (submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent P)

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Line-bundle pullback on a relative curve (A.1.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.1.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the line-bundle
13	pullback functor `π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)` along the projection
14	`π_T : C ×_S T → T` of a relative curve, and the set-valued relative Picard
15	presheaf
16	
17	```
18	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
19	```
20	
21	as a functor on `(Sch/S)^op`.
22	
23	## Status (iter-174 Lane E file-skeleton)
24	
25	This file is the **iter-174 Lane E** file-skeleton: each of the five pinned
26	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
27	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/Picard_LineBundlePullback.tex`) with a
28	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-175+ work after the sibling chapters
29	`Picard_RelativeSpec.lean` (A.1.a) settles its `QcohAlgebra` body, and after
30	`Picard_RelPicFunctor.lean` (A.1.c) lands the étale-sheafification overlay.
31	
32	The 5 pinned declarations are:
33	
34	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.OnProduct` (def, ~5 LOC) — the type of
35	   line bundles on the product `C ×_S T`.
36	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection`
37	   (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the pullback map `Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`.
38	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullback_pullback_eq` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
39	   — composition of pullbacks `g_C^* ∘ π_T^* = π_{T'}^* ∘ g^*` (Stacks 01HG).
40	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup` (def, ~8 LOC)
41	   — well-definedness of the quotient `Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`, encoded as
42	   the substantive `Setoid` on `OnProduct πC πT` that defines the equivalence
43	   relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ (L')⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`.
44	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelPicPresheaf.functorial`
45	   (noncomputable def, ~10 LOC) — for `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, the induced map
46	   `Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) → Pic^♯_{C/S}(T')` factoring `g_C^*` through the quotient.
47	
48	## Note on type expressivity
49	
50	Because Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `Module.Invertible`/`IsInvertible` predicate
51	on `Scheme.Modules` (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for `R : CommRing`),
52	the type `OnProduct` packaging *line bundles* (= invertible sheaves of
53	`O`-modules) on the product cannot yet be carved out of `Scheme.Modules` as a
54	subtype. Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof"
55	we encode `OnProduct` by a typed `sorry` at the type level: the iter-175+ body
56	will instantiate it as a structure pairing a `(pullback πC πT).Modules` carrier
57	with an `IsInvertible` witness once that predicate is in Mathlib (or proven
58	internally as the project-side definition).
59	
60	The 4 pinned theorems/defs reference `OnProduct` as a typed-`sorry` carrier;
61	their signatures are still substantive (each declares a non-tautological claim:
62	a map between line-bundle types, a transitivity-of-pullback equality, an
63	equivalence relation on line bundles, an induced quotient map) and the bodies
64	collapse once `OnProduct` is unpacked.
65	
66	## References
67	
68	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_LineBundlePullback.tex` (444 LOC,
69	5 pins). Source: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", §2 (FGA Explained Ch.9 §9.2),
70	Definitions `df:aPf` (absolute Picard functor) and `df:Pfs` (relative Picard
71	functor). Stacks Project tags 01HG (pullback preserves invertibility),
72	01HH (functoriality of pullback), 01HK (invertible modules), 01CR
73	(Picard group of a scheme).
74	-/
75	
76	set_option autoImplicit false
77	
78	universe u
79	
80	open CategoryTheory Limits
81	
82	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
83	
84	namespace Scheme
85	
86	namespace LineBundle
87	
88	/-! ## §1. Line bundles on a product
89	
90	A *line bundle* on the relative curve `C ×_S T` is an invertible
91	`O_(C ×_S T)`-module, i.e. a sheaf of modules locally free of rank one. In
92	Mathlib `b80f227` the `Scheme.Modules` category is in place
93	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`), but there is no `IsInvertible`
94	predicate on its objects yet (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for rings,
95	in `Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`). iter-187 supplies the missing
96	predicate as a project-side definition via local trivialisation on an affine
97	open cover (see `IsLocallyTrivial` below), and refines `OnProduct` to the
98	corresponding subtype.
99	
100	Blueprint reference: `def:line_bundle_on_product` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
101	functor"; Stacks tag 01HK). -/
102	
103	/-- A sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules `M` is **locally trivial of rank one** if every
104	point `x : X` has an affine open neighbourhood `U` on which the restriction
105	`M|_U` is isomorphic to the structure sheaf-as-module `𝒪_U`. This is the
106	project-side stand-in for the missing Mathlib `IsInvertible` predicate on
107	`Scheme.Modules`: line bundles (invertible sheaves) are exactly the
108	`IsLocallyTrivial` modules, by Stacks tag 01HK / Hartshorne II §6. Local
109	trivialisability is the structural content that survives base change along
110	`f : Y ⟶ X` (Stacks 01HH) and is the source of the invertibility-preservation
111	step for `pullbackAlongProjection`.
112	
113	The witness is bundled as `Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit _)`:
114	the existence of a sheaf-of-modules isomorphism over the affine chart. -/
115	def IsLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
116	  ∀ x : X, ∃ U : X.Opens, x ∈ U ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
117	    Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf)
118	
119	/-- The type of **line bundles on the product** `C ×_S T`: invertible sheaves of
120	`O_(C ×_S T)`-modules, where the product is the categorical pullback of the
121	projections `πC : C ⟶ S` and `πT : T ⟶ S`. In Kleiman's notation with `X = C`
122	and base `S`, this is `Pic_C(T) = Pic(C ×_S T)`, the value of the absolute
123	Picard functor at `T`.
124	
125	iter-187: refined to the subtype of `(Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules` cut out
126	by the project-side predicate `IsLocallyTrivial` (locally trivial of rank one).
127	The carrier is now genuinely the line-bundle subobject of `Scheme.Modules`,
128	matching the blueprint statement `def:line_bundle_on_product`. Iter-186's
129	all-modules carrier is recovered by forgetting the second component. -/
130	def OnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) : Type (u+1) :=
131	  { M : (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules // IsLocallyTrivial M }
132	
133	/-- Projection from a line bundle on the product to its underlying
134	sheaf of modules. -/
135	abbrev OnProduct.carrier {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
136	    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules := L.1
137	
138	/-- A line bundle on the product is locally trivial of rank one. -/
139	lemma OnProduct.isLocallyTrivial {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
140	    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : IsLocallyTrivial L.carrier := L.2
141	
142	/-- **Stacks 01HH (pullback of an invertible sheaf is invertible).** For any
143	morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X` and any locally-trivial sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules
144	`M`, the pulled-back module `f^* M : Y.Modules` is again locally trivial.
145	
146	The proof unfolds an affine open cover trivialising `M`, pulls back along `f`,
147	and observes that `f^*` commutes with restriction along open immersions (the
148	`pullback`/`restrictFunctor` natural isomorphism in
149	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) so that the trivialising chart for
150	`M|_U` becomes a trivialising chart for `(f^* M)|_(f⁻¹ U)`. The full chart-chase
151	involves the natural-isomorphism `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` plus the
152	preservation of `SheafOfModules.unit` under pullback (Mathlib
153	`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackFree`'s
154	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit`). Iter-187 wraps this chase as a named typed sorry
155	(helper-budget = 1); iter-188+ instantiates the chart-chase. -/
156	lemma IsLocallyTrivial.pullback {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) {M : X.Modules}
157	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
158	    IsLocallyTrivial ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) := by
159	  intro y
160	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, eM⟩ := hM (f.base y)
161	  have hyU' : y ∈ f ⁻¹ᵁ U := hxU
162	  obtain ⟨V, hV_aff, hyV, hVU⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset hyU'
163	  refine ⟨V, hyV, hV_aff, ?_⟩
164	  -- Stacks 01HH chart-chase: factor `V.ι ≫ f` through `U` using
165	  -- `Scheme.Hom.resLE`, then transport through `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`
166	  -- and `pullbackComp` to identify the pullback at `V` with the pullback of
167	  -- the unit on `U` to `V`. Close via `eM` and `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
168	  -- (which is an iso since `Opens.map g.base` is final via the
169	  -- `RepresentablyFlat` instance).
170	  obtain ⟨eM⟩ := eM
171	  set g : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := f.resLE U V hVU with hg_def
172	  have hg_comp : g ≫ U.ι = V.ι ≫ f := Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι f hVU
173	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final :=
174	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
175	  refine ⟨?_⟩
176	  -- 1: ((pullback f).obj M).restrict V.ι ≅ (pullback V.ι).obj ((pullback f).obj M)
177	  let i1 :=
178	    (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M)
179	  -- 2: ≅ (pullback (V.ι ≫ f)).obj M
180	  let i2 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι f).app M
181	  -- 3: ≅ (pullback (g ≫ U.ι)).obj M  (using V.ι ≫ f = g ≫ U.ι)
182	  let i3 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hg_comp.symm).app M
183	  -- 4: ≅ (pullback g).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
184	  let i4 := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g U.ι).symm).app M
185	  -- 5: ≅ (pullback g).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
186	  let i5 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso
187	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M)
188	  -- 6: ≅ (pullback g).obj (unit_U)
189	  let i6 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso eM
190	  -- 7: ≅ unit_V  (pullback of unit is unit via the Mathlib morphism, which is
191	  -- iso since the underlying `Opens.map g.base` is Final)
192	  let i7 := asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
193	  exact i1 ≪≫ i2 ≪≫ i3 ≪≫ i4 ≪≫ i5 ≪≫ i6 ≪≫ i7
194	
195	/-! ## §2. The pullback functor along the projection
196	
197	For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ Z` and an invertible `O_Z`-module `M`, the
198	pullback `f^* M` is an invertible `O_Y`-module (the pullback of an
199	open-cover-trivialisation of `M` trivialises `f^* M`, and the pullback of a free
200	module of rank one is free of rank one — Stacks tag 01HH). Specialising to the
201	projection `π_T = pullback.snd πC πT : C ×_S T ⟶ T` gives the pullback map
202	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`. Mathlib's underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
203	functor (in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) supplies the pullback on
204	the full category of sheaves of modules; iter-175+ will restrict it to
205	invertible sheaves once the `OnProduct` carrier is unpacked.
206	
207	Blueprint reference: `def:pullback_along_projection` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag
208	01HH). -/
209	
210	/-- **Pullback along the projection** `π_T : C ×_S T ⟶ T` of a relative
211	curve. Sends a *line bundle on `T`* — i.e. an `O_T`-module `N` equipped with a
212	proof `hN : IsLocallyTrivial N` — to the line bundle `π_T^* N` on `C ×_S T`,
213	induced by the `Scheme.Modules.pullback` functor on `Scheme.Modules` together
214	with the preservation step `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` (Stacks 01HH).
215	
216	iter-187: refined to consume the `OnProduct`-style subtype on the source: the
217	underlying carrier is the direct application of Mathlib's
218	`Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)` functor at `N`, and the
219	locally-trivial witness comes from the project-side helper
220	`IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` (Stacks 01HH, named typed sorry pending the affine
221	chart chase). -/
222	noncomputable def pullbackAlongProjection {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
223	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (N : T.Modules) (hN : IsLocallyTrivial N) :
224	    OnProduct πC πT :=
225	  ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N,
226	    hN.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)⟩
227	
228	/-! ## §3. Composition of pullbacks
229	
230	The composition law for line-bundle pullback (Stacks tag 01HG) says that for any
231	composable morphisms `f : X ⟶ Y, g : Y ⟶ Z` and any line bundle `M ∈ Pic(Z)`,
232	the natural isomorphism `(g ∘ f)^* M ≅ f^* g^* M` makes the pullback functor
233	into a (pseudo-)functor `Sch^op ⥤ Cat`.
234	
235	Specialised to the relative curve setting: given `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, the
236	base-change morphism `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` satisfies
237	`π_T ∘ g_C = g ∘ π_{T'}` (definition of the fiber product). Therefore on
238	line bundles `g_C^* ∘ π_T^* = π_{T'}^* ∘ g^*`, with the equality realised as the
239	canonical natural isomorphism of pullback functors via the pseudo-functoriality
240	of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` (Mathlib `Modules.pullbackComp`).
241	
242	Blueprint reference: `lem:pullback_compose` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
243	
244	/-- **Composition of line-bundle pullbacks** along the projection. For a base
245	scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, test objects `T, T'` over `S`
246	via `πT : T ⟶ S` and `πT' : T' ⟶ S`, and a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`
247	(encoded by the hypothesis `πT' = g ≫ πT`), set
248	`g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T`
249	(the base-change morphism, given by `pullback.map`). On the underlying
250	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` functors the two routes through the canonical
251	naturality square give canonically isomorphic objects:
252	```
253	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)
254	  T.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T).Modules
255	       │                                                              │
256	       │ Scheme.Modules.pullback g            Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C
257	       ▼                                                              ▼
258	 T'.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T').Modules
259	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')
260	```
261	i.e. for any `N : T.Modules`,
262	```
263	(Modules.pullback g_C).obj ((Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N)
264	  ≅
265	(Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')).obj ((Modules.pullback g).obj N).
266	```
267	
268	This natural isomorphism is the substantive content of the
269	"line-bundle pullback functor is well-behaved under composition" fact
270	(Stacks tag 01HG). At the level of the `OnProduct` carrier (which is a typed
271	`sorry` in iter-174; see §1) it descends to equality of isomorphism classes
272	`[g_C^* π_T^* N] = [π_{T'}^* g^* N] ∈ OnProduct πC πT'`.
273	
274	iter-186: closed via the chain
275	1. `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g_C (pullback.snd πC πT)` (functor iso applied at `N`)
276	2. `Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr` for the equation `g_C ≫ snd πC πT = snd πC πT' ≫ g`
277	   (`pullback.lift_snd` on the `pullback.map` lift).
278	3. `(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (pullback.snd πC πT') g).symm` (applied at `N`). -/
279	theorem pullback_pullback_eq {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
280	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T)
281	    (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) (N : T.Modules) :
282	    Nonempty
283	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
284	            (Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
285	              (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg]))).obj
286	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N) ≅
287	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT')).obj
288	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N)) := by
289	  set g_C : Limits.pullback πC πT' ⟶ Limits.pullback πC πT :=
290	    Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
291	      (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg])
292	  have key : g_C ≫ Limits.pullback.snd πC πT = Limits.pullback.snd πC πT' ≫ g :=
293	    Limits.pullback.lift_snd _ _ _
294	  refine ⟨?_⟩
295	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g_C (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).app N ≪≫ ?_
296	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr key).app N ≪≫ ?_
297	  exact ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT') g).app N).symm
298	
299	end LineBundle

301	namespace RelPicPresheaf
302	
303	/-! ## §4. The relative Picard quotient (set-valued)
304	
305	The relative Picard presheaf `Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`
306	quotients the line-bundle group of the product by the subgroup pulled back from
307	the base. As a quotient of abelian groups it is a quotient set; equivalently,
308	the equivalence relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` is captured by a
309	`Setoid` on the line-bundle type `OnProduct πC πT`. The quotient
310	`Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the underlying set of the relative
311	Picard presheaf at `T`.
312	
313	The "preimage_subgroup" name follows the Kleiman/Stacks convention: the
314	quotient is by the preimage `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)`, treated as a
315	subgroup (image of a group homomorphism); the project-side Lean encoding
316	extracts the *Setoid* that the quotient construction takes.
317	
318	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined` (Kleiman §2,
319	Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
320	
321	/-- **Well-definedness of the relative Picard quotient as a `Setoid`.**
322	
323	The equivalence relation on `OnProduct πC πT` defining the relative Picard
324	presheaf
325	```
326	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
327	```
328	is the relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`. This relation is reflexive
329	(`L ⊗ L⁻¹ = O_(C ×_S T) = π_T^* O_T ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`), symmetric (the inverse of
330	a line bundle in `π_T^* Pic(T)` is in `π_T^* Pic(T)`), and transitive
331	(`L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` and `L' ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` give
332	`L ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`); hence is an equivalence relation, encoded by a
333	`Setoid` on `OnProduct πC πT`.
334	
335	The substantive content is the well-definedness of the quotient: the canonical
336	projection `OnProduct πC πT → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the
337	set-theoretic underlying map of the relative Picard presheaf at `T`.
338	
339	iter-186: with `OnProduct := (pullback πC πT).Modules`, the carrier is the
340	modules category. We encode the **iso-class setoid** `L ~ L' ↔ Nonempty (L ≅ L')`.
341	This is a substantive simplification: it identifies isomorphic modules
342	(giving the underlying set of `Pic(C ×_S T)` once invertibility is imposed),
343	but does not yet quotient by the pullback subgroup `π_T^* Pic(T)`. The full
344	quotient requires a tensor-product structure on `Scheme.Modules` (Mathlib
345	ships `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` but no monoidal structure on
346	`Scheme.Modules` directly at `b80f227`) plus an inverse for invertibles;
347	iter-187+: refine to `L ~ L' ↔ ∃ N : T.Modules, Nonempty (L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ≅
348	pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N)` once both are available. -/
349	noncomputable def preimage_subgroup {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
350	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
351	    Setoid (LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) where
352	  r L L' := Nonempty (L.carrier ≅ L'.carrier)
353	  iseqv :=
354	    { refl := fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩
355	      symm := fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩
356	      trans := fun ⟨e₁⟩ ⟨e₂⟩ => ⟨e₁ ≪≫ e₂⟩ }
357	
358	/-! ## §5. Naturality in the test scheme — the relative Picard presheaf as a functor
359	
360	For a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S` (encoded by the equality `πT' = g ≫ πT`),
361	the base-change `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` induces a map
362	`g_C^* : Pic(C ×_S T) → Pic(C ×_S T')` on line bundles, which (by
363	`lem:pullback_compose` = `pullback_pullback_eq` above) sends the subgroup
364	`π_T^* Pic(T)` into `π_{T'}^* Pic(T')`. Therefore it descends to a unique map
365	on quotients
366	```
367	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T'),    [L] ↦ [g_C^* L],
368	```
369	making `Pic^♯_{C/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` a presheaf.
370	
371	For the iter-174 file-skeleton, we pin the **object-level** functorial action
372	(i.e. the induced map of quotient sets for a single test morphism `g`). The
373	full `Functor` packaging (with the identity / composition laws supplied as
374	`map_id`, `map_comp` fields) is iter-175+ work once `OnProduct` and the
375	`preimage_subgroup` carrier are unpacked.
376	
377	Blueprint reference: `thm:pullback_natural` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
378	functor" + Def. `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
379	
380	/-- **The relative Picard presheaf is functorial in the test scheme.**
381	
382	Object-level functorial action: for any morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, there
383	is a canonical map
384	```
385	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T')
386	```
387	of quotient sets, factoring `g_C^*` through `preimage_subgroup πC πT` and
388	`preimage_subgroup πC πT'`. On representatives, `g^♯ [L] = [g_C^* L]` where
389	`g_C := id_C ×_S g`. The factorisation through the quotient is well-defined
390	because `g_C^*` sends `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)` into
391	`π_{T'}^* Pic(T') ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T')` (this is the commutative-square content of
392	`pullback_pullback_eq`).
393	
394	iter-186: with the iso-class setoid `preimage_subgroup`, `Quotient.lift`
395	on `L ↦ ⟦(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).obj L⟧` is well-defined because
396	functors preserve isomorphisms. The identity / composition laws giving the
397	full `Functor (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` packaging follow from `pullbackId` /
398	`pullbackComp` and are iter-187+ work (they currently aren't needed by any
399	downstream consumer). -/
400	noncomputable def functorial {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
401	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T) (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) :
402	    Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT) → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT') :=
403	  let g_C : Limits.pullback πC πT' ⟶ Limits.pullback πC πT :=
404	    Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
405	      (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg])
406	  Quotient.lift
407	    (fun L : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT =>
408	      Quotient.mk (preimage_subgroup πC πT')
409	        (⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).obj L.carrier,
410	          L.isLocallyTrivial.pullback g_C⟩ : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT'))
411	    (fun _ _ ⟨e⟩ => Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).mapIso e⟩)
412	
413	end RelPicPresheaf
414	
415	end Scheme
416	
417	end AlgebraicGeometry

10	# Milne Theorem 3.2: a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian
11	variety extends (A.4.c)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.c** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
14	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the single
15	theorem of Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3, Theorem 3.2:
16	**a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends,
17	uniquely, to a regular morphism**. This is the input that the Albanese
18	universal-property build (A.4.d, `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`) consumes when
19	promoting the symmetric-product rational map
20	`C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism `J → A`.
21	
22	Milne's proof is two lines: combine Theorem 3.1 (the codim-$\geq 2$ extension
23	result for rational maps from a normal variety to a complete variety —
24	project-side `thm:codim_one_extension` of
25	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, A.4.a) with Lemma 3.3 (the
26	pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy structure for rational maps into a group variety
27	— project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` of the same chapter). The
28	combination forces the indeterminacy locus of `f` to be simultaneously of
29	codimension `≥ 2` and pure codimension `1`, hence empty, so `f` is everywhere
30	defined.
31	
32	## Status (iter-175 Lane J file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-175 Lane J** file-skeleton: the single pinned
35	declaration carries the intended substantive type signature (matching the
36	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50) with a `sorry`

45	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
46	   — Milne Theorem 3.2: for `X` a smooth (= nonsingular) variety over an
47	   algebraically closed field `k̄`, `A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, and

51	## Note on type expressivity
52	
53	The signature is encoded against Mathlib's existing
54	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` (a partial-map equivalence class on
55	dense opens) and `Scheme.Hom.toRationalMap` (the canonical "regular morphism
56	yields a rational map" coercion). The substantive claim is the existence
57	and uniqueness of a `g : X.left ⟶ A.left` with `g.toRationalMap = f`. The

62	## Abelian-variety conventions

64	Throughout the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
65	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
66	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
67	
68	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
69	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
70	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
71	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
72	
73	A **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** (in the convention of
74	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1) is an
75	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:

77	- `[Smooth X.hom]`,
78	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
79	- `[IsSeparated X.hom]`,
80	- `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
81	- `[IsIntegral X.left]`,
82	- `[IsReduced X.left]`.
83	
84	These four-plus-two instances will normally be supplied by `inferInstance` at
85	the call site.

106	/-! ## Milne Theorem 3.2 — rational maps to an abelian variety extend
107	
108	Let `k̄` be an algebraically closed field. Let `X` be a nonsingular variety
109	over `k̄` (smooth, geometrically irreducible, separated, locally of finite
110	type, integral, reduced). Let `A` be an abelian variety over `k̄`
111	(a group object that is proper, smooth, and geometrically irreducible). For
112	every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` there is a *unique* regular morphism
113	`g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
114	
115	Milne's argument (`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1.4, Theorem 3.2):
116	let `U ⊆ X` be a maximal open of definition of `f` and `Z := X ∖ U`.
117	By the codim-$\geq 2$ extension theorem (Theorem 3.1, project-side
118	`thm:codim_one_extension`) the closed subset `Z` has codimension `≥ 2`.
119	By the pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy lemma for maps into a group variety
120	(Lemma 3.3, project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy`), `Z` is either
121	empty or of pure codimension `1`. Both conditions force `Z = ∅`, so `U = X`
122	and `f` admits a regular representative on all of `X`. Uniqueness is the
123	standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle (cf.
124	`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two regular morphisms `X ⟶ A` that
125	agree on a dense open of `X` agree everywhere.
126	
127	Blueprint reference: `thm:rational_map_to_av_extends` (Milne, *Abelian
128	Varieties*, Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17). The pin in the chapter is at
129	`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50.
130	
131	iter-176+ proof skeleton:
132	1. Pick a `PartialMap` representative `φ : X.PartialMap A` of `f` (using
133	   `Scheme.RationalMap.toPartialMap`, which exists for `X` reduced and `A`
134	   separated; both hold from the abelian-variety + variety instances).
135	2. Show `φ.domain = ⊤` by combining `thm:codim_one_extension` (codim `≥ 2`
136	   complement) with `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (complement empty or
137	   pure codim `1`).
138	3. Read off `g := φ.hom` (the morphism on the full domain) and verify
139	   `g.toRationalMap = f`.
140	4. Uniqueness: two `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ A` with `g₁.toRationalMap = g₂.toRationalMap`
141	   agree on a dense open of `X`, so by
142	   `AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` and reducedness of `X` they agree
143	   everywhere. -/
144	
145	/-- **Mathlib gap (Stacks `034V`/`02G4`)**: a scheme smooth over a
146	reduced base is reduced. Used to derive `IsReduced A.left` in
147	`av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` below. Typed sorry; the gap is
148	the scheme-level packaging of the Stacks lemma.
149	**Demoted from inline sorry per lean-auditor iter-196 must-fix**: isolates
150	the `sorryAx` to a single named declaration. -/
151	private theorem isReduced_of_smooth_over_field
152	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
153	    (A : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
154	    [Smooth A.hom] :
155	    IsReduced A.left := sorry
156	
157	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (1/2): integrality of the abelian variety.**
158	
159	For an abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` —
160	encoded in the project as `A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying
161	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` —
162	the underlying scheme `A.left` is integral.
163	
164	**Derivation.** The proof has three steps:
165	
166	1. `Spec k̄` is a singleton scheme (Mathlib instance
167	   `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf` provides `Unique`,
168	   which yields both `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty`).
169	2. `GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom + Spec k̄ a single point ⟹ IrreducibleSpace A.left`
170	   via Mathlib's `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`.
171	3. `IrreducibleSpace + IsReduced ⟹ IsIntegral` via Mathlib's
172	   `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`.
173	
174	The substantive Mathlib gap is the implication `Smooth A.hom ⟹ IsReduced A.left`
175	(at the project's pinned commit, Mathlib's `Smooth` morphism class does not
176	ship a `IsReduced` consequence for the source over a reduced base). The
177	gap is isolated to the single `IsReduced A.left` hypothesis in the proof,
178	which is left as a `sorry`; all other steps close axiom-clean.
179	
180	When Mathlib ships the bridge (Stacks `034V` / `02G4` over a field
181	direction), the single `sorry` discharges and this helper becomes
182	axiom-clean automatically.
183	
184	This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor of the iter-179 helper
185	`av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, splitting its `IsIntegral A.left`
186	conjunct into a self-contained named lemma that does not depend on the
187	rational map `f`. -/
188	private theorem av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred
189	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
190	    (A : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
191	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
192	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] :
193	    IsIntegral A.left := by
194	  -- Step 1: `Spec k̄` has a unique point (Mathlib instance
195	  -- `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf`); both
196	  -- `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty` follow.
197	  haveI : Subsingleton (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
198	  haveI : Nonempty (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
199	  -- Step 2: geometric irreducibility over a singleton base gives `IrreducibleSpace`.
200	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace A.left :=
201	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton A.hom
202	  -- Step 3 (Mathlib gap): smoothness over a field forces reducedness of the
203	  -- source. Named typed-sorry helper `isReduced_of_smooth_over_field` isolates
204	  -- the `sorryAx` per lean-auditor iter-196 must-fix.
205	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := isReduced_of_smooth_over_field A
206	  exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced A.left
207	
208	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (2/2): codim-1-indeterminacy-freeness from
209	Milne's two-input argument.**
210	
211	For a rational map `f : X ⇢ A` from a nonsingular variety `X` to an
212	abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
213	codim-1 point of `X` lies in `f.domain` (`CodimOneFree f`).
214	
215	**Derivation (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, proof of Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17).**
216	Two project-side inputs combine to force the indeterminacy locus empty:
217	
218	1. `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 in
219	   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): for a rational map from a smooth
220	   integral variety to a *group* variety, the indeterminacy locus is
221	   either empty or pure codim 1.
222	2. The *codim-≥2 conclusion* of Milne Theorem 3.1 (the unbundled half of
223	   `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`):
224	   for a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety,
225	   the indeterminacy locus has codim `≥ 2`.
226	
227	Combining (1) ∨ (2): pure codim 1 contradicts codim `≥ 2`, so the
228	indeterminacy locus is empty; trivially every point — and in particular
229	every codim-1 point — lies in `f.domain`, so `CodimOneFree f`.
230	
231	**Status of the substantive content.** The codim-≥2 conclusion of
232	Milne 3.1 is not currently exposed by the project as a standalone lemma;
233	it is encapsulated inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s body (which
234	is itself `sorry` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`). Once the project
235	exposes a lemma `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` separating
236	out the codim-≥2 conclusion, this helper closes via the two-line
237	combination above. Until then it is left as a named `sorry` whose
238	docstring exhaustively records the combination strategy, replacing the
239	iter-179 conjunction-level `sorry` with a more targeted one.
240	
241	This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor's split: the `CodimOneFree f`
242	conjunct is split off into a self-contained named lemma with the
243	substantive missing content localised here. -/
244	private theorem av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy
245	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
246	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
247	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
248	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
249	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
250	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
251	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
252	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
253	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
254	    CodimOneFree f := by
255	  -- **Iter-182 structural refactor.** Materialise the variety-package
256	  -- instances on `A.left` needed by Milne Lemma 3.3
257	  -- (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): `IsIntegral` from the
258	  -- split helper `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred`; `IsReduced` from
259	  -- `isReduced_of_isIntegral`; `IsSeparated` from `IsProper`;
260	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType` from `Smooth`.
261	  haveI : IsIntegral A.left := av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A
262	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
263	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
264	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
265	  -- Apply Milne Lemma 3.3: the indeterminacy locus is empty or every point
266	  -- of it lies in the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
267	  have hdisj := indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme f
268	  intro x hx
269	  rcases hdisj with hempty | hpure
270	  · -- **Branch 1 (CLOSED).** Empty indeterminacy locus: every point of `X`
271	    -- — in particular `x` — lies in `f.domain` (no codim-1 obstruction).
272	    by_contra hnotin
273	    have hxZ : x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f := hnotin
274	    rw [hempty] at hxZ
275	    exact hxZ.elim
276	  · -- **Branch 2 (PROJECT GAP).** The disjunct gives, for any `x ∈ Z(f)`,
277	    -- a codim-1 generic point `z` with `x ∈ closure {z}`. To prove
278	    -- `x ∈ f.domain` for our codim-1 `x`, contrapositive: assume
279	    -- `x ∈ Z(f)`. The disjunct produces a `z` with `coheight z = 1` and
280	    -- `x ∈ closure {z}` — but for a codim-1 `x`, sobriety of the scheme
281	    -- forces `z = x` (chains of length 1 starting at `z` and ending at a
282	    -- specialisation `x` of equal coheight collapse in a sober T₀ space),
283	    -- so `x = z` is a codim-1 point of `Z(f)`. The contradiction needs
284	    -- the **codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1** (project-side
285	    -- `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s unbundled half), which is
286	    -- currently encapsulated inside that theorem's `sorry`'d body in
287	    -- `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` rather than exposed as a separate
288	    -- lemma. Once a lemma
289	    -- `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete : ∀ z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f, coheight z ≥ 2`
290	    -- lands in `CodimOneExtension.lean`, branch 2 closes via:
291	    --   `by_contra hnotin; obtain ⟨z, hz1, hxz⟩ := hpure x hnotin;`
292	    --   `obtain rfl := sober_eq_of_specializes_of_coheight_eq hxz hx hz1;`
293	    --   `exact absurd hx (lt_irrefl 1 ∘ ... ∘ codimGe2 hnotin)`.
294	    sorry
295	
296	/-- **Iter-182 documentation helper: branch-1 of `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy`.**
297	The empty-indeterminacy branch of Milne Lemma 3.3 closes `CodimOneFree`
298	without depending on the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1. Exposed as a
299	standalone lemma so the structural split survives any future refactor of
300	the parent helper. Axiom-clean. -/
301	private theorem codimOneFree_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty
302	    {X Y : Scheme.{u}} {f : X.RationalMap Y}
303	    (h : indeterminacyLocus f = ∅) : CodimOneFree f := by
304	  intro x _
305	  by_contra hnotin
306	  have hxZ : x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f := hnotin
307	  rw [h] at hxZ
308	  exact hxZ.elim
309	
310	/-- **Iter-179 Lane E + iter-180 Lane G: the two missing project inputs
311	for `extend_to_av`.**
312	
313	The conjunction of the two named iter-180 Lane G splits:
314	`av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` (the `IsIntegral A.left` conjunct, with
315	its `IsReduced A.left` Mathlib gap isolated) and
316	`av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` (the `CodimOneFree f` conjunct, with its
317	codim-≥2-from-Milne-3.1 gap isolated).
318	
319	This wrapper preserves the iter-179 call-site API (`extend_to_av` invokes
320	the conjunction in one `obtain`) while routing the proof to the two
321	narrower lemmas. The body itself is axiom-clean — all substantive content
322	is localised in the two split helpers above. -/
323	private theorem av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree
324	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
325	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
326	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
327	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
328	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
329	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
330	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
331	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
332	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
333	    IsIntegral A.left ∧ CodimOneFree f :=
334	  ⟨av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A,
335	    av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy f⟩
336	
337	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.2 — a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an
338	abelian variety extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism.**
339	
340	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, and
341	`A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` has a
342	*unique* regular extension `g : X ⟶ A` (i.e. `g.toRationalMap = f`).
343	
344	This is the input the Albanese universal-property build
345	(`Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`, A.4.d) consumes when promoting the
346	symmetric-product rational map `C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism
347	`J → A`.
348	
349	**Iter-179 Lane E body.** The proof is the standard one-line reduction of
350	Milne's two-line argument:
351	
352	1. Apply `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (Milne Theorem 3.1, project-side
353	   `thm:codim_one_extension` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): given a
354	   `CodimOneFree` rational map from a smooth integral variety to a complete
355	   variety, the map extends uniquely to a regular morphism. This requires
356	   the variety-package instances on `A.left` (separated, locally of finite
357	   type, integral, reduced) in addition to the four AV instances supplied
358	   by the caller.
359	
360	2. Discharge `CodimOneFree f` by invoking the helper
361	   `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, which combines
362	   `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure
363	   codim 1 or empty indeterminacy for grpScheme targets) with the codim-≥2
364	   half of Milne 3.1. Both pieces are documented in that helper's
365	   docstring; the helper carries the single `sorry` that captures the
366	   remaining math content the project spec does not yet expose.
367	
368	3. Materialize the remaining variety-package instances on `A.left`:
369	   `IsSeparated A.hom` from `IsProper A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
370	   `LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom` from `Smooth A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
371	   `IsReduced A.left` from `IsIntegral A.left` (Mathlib instance
372	   `isReduced_of_isIntegral`).
373	
374	The proof body therefore introduces NO new inline `sorry`: all missing
375	content is concentrated in the single named helper above. -/
376	theorem extend_to_av
377	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
378	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
379	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
380	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
381	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
382	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
383	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
384	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
385	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
386	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ A.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
387	  -- Step 3: materialise the missing variety-package instances on A.left.
388	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
389	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
390	  obtain ⟨hint, hcod⟩ := av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree f
391	  haveI := hint
392	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
393	  -- Steps 1–2: apply Milne Theorem 3.1 with the `CodimOneFree` discharged.
394	  exact extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth f hcod
395	
396	end RationalMap
397	
398	end Scheme
399	
400	end AlgebraicGeometry
