36	
37	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
38	
39	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
40	
41	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
42	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
43	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
44	
45	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
46	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
47	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
48	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
49	
50	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
51	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
52	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
53	
54	/-- `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f` via the
55	composition `kbar →+* ↥(𝒜 0) →+* Away 𝒜 f`. Mathlib only ships
56	`Algebra (𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)`; this instance bridges the
57	remaining `kbar →+* 𝒜 0` algebra map shipped via `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`.
58	Required for `TensorProduct kbar (Away _ _) _` to synthesize `CommRing`/`Algebra kbar`. -/
59	noncomputable instance algebraKbarAway (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
60	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :
61	    Algebra kbar
62	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) :=
63	  Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
64	
65	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
66	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
67	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
68	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
69	
70	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
71	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
72	irreducibility instances. -/
73	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
74	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
75	
76	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
77	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
78	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
80	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
81	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
82	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
83	
84	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
85	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
86	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
87	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
88	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
89	
90	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
91	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
92	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
93	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
94	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
95	
96	The chain:
97	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
98	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
99	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
100	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
101	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
102	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
103	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
104	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
105	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
106	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
108	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
109	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
110	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
111	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
112	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
113	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
114	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
115	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
116	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
117	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
118	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
119	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
120	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
121	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
122	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
123	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
124	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
125	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
126	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
127	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
128	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
129	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
130	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
131	    · intro x y h
132	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
133	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
134	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
135	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
136	      apply Subtype.ext
137	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
138	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
139	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
140	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
141	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
142	      exact key
143	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
144	  -- proper.
145	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
146	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
147	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
148	    exact hbij
149	  infer_instance
150	
151	/-! ### Project-local Mathlib supplement — MvPolynomial as standard smooth algebra
152	
153	This subsection ships an axiom-clean construction of the canonical submersive
154	presentation of `MvPolynomial ι R` as an `R`-algebra (no relations, generators
155	indexed by `ι`). Mathlib does not ship a direct instance
156	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)`
157	(per iter-182 prover scout); we build it here from the underlying
158	`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation` machinery. This is a pure
159	`Mathlib`-supplement: it depends only on `Mathlib`, and is independent
160	of the rest of the project.
161	-/
162	
163	/-- The canonical `Generators` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R` indexed by `α`,
164	with `val := MvPolynomial.X` and section `σ' := id`. -/
165	noncomputable def mvPolyGenerators (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (α : Type w) :
166	    Algebra.Generators R (MvPolynomial α R) α :=
167	  Algebra.Generators.ofSurjective MvPolynomial.X <|
168	    fun s => ⟨s, MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left_apply s⟩
169	
170	/-- The canonical `Presentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R` with no relations
171	(`σ = PEmpty`) and generators indexed by `α`. The kernel of the natural
172	`aeval X` map is `⊥` since `aeval X` is the identity. -/
173	noncomputable def mvPolyPresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (α : Type w) :
174	    Algebra.Presentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
175	  __ := mvPolyGenerators R α
176	  relation := PEmpty.elim
177	  span_range_relation_eq_ker := by
178	    simp only [Set.range_eq_empty, Ideal.span_empty]
179	    rw [Algebra.Generators.ker_eq_ker_aeval_val]
180	    change ⊥ = RingHom.ker (MvPolynomial.aeval (R := R) (MvPolynomial.X : α → MvPolynomial α R))
181	    rw [MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left]
182	    ext x
183	    simp [RingHom.mem_ker]
184	
185	/-- The canonical `PreSubmersivePresentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R`.
186	The differential is on the zero module `PEmpty → P.Ring`. -/
187	noncomputable def mvPolyPreSubmersivePresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R]
188	    (α : Type w) :
189	    Algebra.PreSubmersivePresentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
190	  __ := mvPolyPresentation R α
191	  map := PEmpty.elim
192	  map_inj := fun a _ _ => PEmpty.elim a
193	
194	/-- The canonical `SubmersivePresentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R`.
195	The Jacobian is the determinant of the zero-sized matrix, which is `1` (a unit). -/
196	noncomputable def mvPolySubmersivePresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R]
197	    (α : Type w) :
198	    Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
199	  __ := mvPolyPreSubmersivePresentation R α
200	  jacobian_isUnit := by
201	    rw [Algebra.PreSubmersivePresentation.jacobian_eq_jacobiMatrix_det]
202	    simp [Matrix.det_isEmpty]
203	
204	/-- **Mathlib supplement**: `MvPolynomial (Fin n) R` is `R`-standard smooth of
205	relative dimension `n`. Built from `mvPolySubmersivePresentation` with
206	dimension `Nat.card (Fin n) - Nat.card PEmpty = n`. -/
207	instance mvPolynomialFin_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
208	    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ) :
209	    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :=
210	  (mvPolySubmersivePresentation R (Fin n)).isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension <| by
211	    simp [Algebra.Presentation.dimension]
212	
213	/-! ### `GeometricallyIrreducible` instance -/
214	
215	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
216	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
217	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
218	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
219	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
220	  sorry
221	
222	/-! ### The 2-chart affine cover of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` -/
223	
224	/-- **Per-chart degree-1 homogeneity witness** for the affine cover of
225	`ProjectiveLineBarScheme` by `D₊(X 0)` and `D₊(X 1)`. Hoisted to top-level
226	out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> simp` proof inside
227	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (per `analogies/gmscaling-cover-bridge.md`
228	Step 1) so the kernel doesn't `whnf` tactic-built proof closures during
229	downstream defeq in `gmScalingP1`. -/
230	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg
231	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
232	    ∀ i, (![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1]) i ∈
233	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar ((![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i) :=
234	  fun i => by
235	    fin_cases i <;> simp [Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
236	      MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X]
237	
238	/-- **Positive-degree witness** for the affine cover indexing. Hoisted to
239	top-level out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos` proof
240	inside `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` for the same defeq reasons as
241	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`. -/
242	lemma projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm :
243	    ∀ i, 0 < (![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i :=
244	  fun i => by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos
245	
246	/-- **The 2-chart affine open cover of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`** by `D₊(X 0)` and
247	`D₊(X 1)`. Specialises `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to the family
248	`![X 0, X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` with `m := ![1, 1]`.
249	
250	The non-trivial bit is `hf`: the irrelevant ideal `(X 0, X 1)` is contained in
251	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
252	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
253	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
254	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
255	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
256	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
257	    (m := ![1, 1])
258	    (![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1])
259	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar)
260	    projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm
261	    (by
262	      classical
263	      intro p hp
264	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
265	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
266	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by
267	        have := hp
268	        rw [show DirectSum.decompose (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) p 0
269	            = ⟨MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p,
270	                MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_mem 0 p⟩ from Subtype.ext
271	          (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)] at this
272	        exact this
273	      have h0 : MvPolynomial.coeff 0 p = 0 := by
274	        rw [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero] at hp'
275	        exact MvPolynomial.C_injective _ _ (hp'.trans MvPolynomial.C_0.symm)
276	      rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]
277	      refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd ↦ ?_
278	      have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff d p ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hd
279	      have hd_ne : d ≠ 0 := fun heq => hcoeff (heq ▸ h0)
280	      have hd_nonzero : d 0 ≠ 0 ∨ d 1 ≠ 0 := by
281	        by_contra h
282	        push Not at h
283	        apply hd_ne
284	        ext k
285	        fin_cases k
286	        · simpa using h.1
287	        · simpa using h.2
288	      rcases hd_nonzero with h0' | h1'
289	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
290	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
291	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h0')
292	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
293	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨0, rfl⟩)
294	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
295	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
296	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h1')
297	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
298	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨1, rfl⟩))
299	
300	-- NOTE iter-197: relocated to AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/ChartIso.lean per BareScheme smoothness-relocation refactor.
301	
302	end AlgebraicGeometry
303	
304	end

14	/-!
15	# Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme
16	
17	The cotangent space at the identity of a smooth proper geometrically
18	irreducible group scheme `G` over a base field `k`, defined as a
19	`k`-vector space. Mathematically, this is the pullback of the relative
20	cotangent sheaf `Ω_{G/k}` along the identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`;
21	the iter-130 Lean realisation (Replacement (B), per
22	`analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`) computes it as the base change to
23	`k` of the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` on a
24	smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image.
25	
26	This is piece (i.a) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (see
27	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i): sub-lemma
28	decomposition for iter-128+ build" and STRATEGY.md § M2.body-pile).
29	
30	This file contains the definition `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` below, the
31	structural-shape acceptance lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars`
32	below, and the companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq`
33	below (rank = relative dimension `n` from
34	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`, closed iter-132).
35	
36	## Status (iter-132 close: rank lemma)
37	
38	`AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is fully constructed
39	(no `sorry`). The iter-128 body (presheaf evaluated at the top open then
40	extended scalars to `k`) was found in iter-129 to compute the zero
41	`k`-module for every `G` in the target class with `n ≥ 1` (`Spec` of a
42	field is a single point, so the pulled-back presheaf collapses to `k`
43	and the Kähler module of the identity `k → k` is zero). The iter-130
44	fix-up replaced it with **Replacement (B): affine-chart base change** —
45	extract an affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image
46	on which the algebraic Kähler module is free of rank `n` (via
47	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`), then base-change to `k` along the
48	identity section restricted to `V`. The iter-130 realisation passed
49	through `Classical.choice` to bridge `Prop` and `Type`, which left the
50	elaborated body with `Classical.choice` as its outermost head symbol —
51	opaque to downstream `unfold`/`whnf`. The **iter-131 fix-up** refactors
52	the body to a pure-term `noncomputable def`: `let`-bindings of
53	`Classical.choose`/`.choose_spec` on the `smooth_locally_free_omega`
54	existential expose `U, V, e, ψV` as named-but-opaque accessors, while
55	the outer expression is the explicit
56	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`. The
57	signature, set in iter-129 (free `{n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`
58	binder), is preserved. The iter-131 body shape is witnessed by
59	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below.
60	
61	The **iter-132 prover lane** closed the companion rank lemma
62	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244 below) against the
63	iter-131 body via a parallel `Classical.choose`-chain extraction on
64	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, reducing through
65	`Module.finrank_baseChange` + `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq` to pin the
66	`k`-finrank of the cotangent space at the identity to the relative
67	dimension `n` from the `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
68

88	-- Mathlib's `Algebra.TensorProduct` ships only the LEFT algebra instance on
89	-- `B₁ ⊗[k] B₂`; the symmetric right-algebra is a `local instance` inside
90	-- `Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct`. Re-enable it here so the canonical
91	-- `Algebra.IsPushout k B₁ B₂ (B₁ ⊗[k] B₂)` instance from Mathlib resolves.
92	attribute [local instance] Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
93	
94	namespace GrpObj
95	
96	/-- Chart-algebra (α): the affine pullback `Spec B₁ ×_(Spec k) Spec B₂ =
97	Spec (B₁ ⊗_k B₂)` carries the canonical `Algebra.IsPushout k B₁ B₂
98	(B₁ ⊗[k] B₂)` square at the ring level. Refined iter-146 to its real
99	signature (the algebra-level core of the blueprint's three-step
100	chart-scheme bridge); discharged by `inferInstance` after re-enabling
101	the local right-algebra instance. -/
102	theorem algebra_isPushout_of_affine_product
103	    (k B₁ B₂ : Type*) [CommRing k] [CommRing B₁] [CommRing B₂]
104	    [Algebra k B₁] [Algebra k B₂] :
105	    Algebra.IsPushout k B₁ B₂ (TensorProduct k B₁ B₂) :=
106	  inferInstance

108	end GrpObj
109	
110	/-- (FT.3 base case) The universal Kähler derivation does not kill the
111	transcendental generator: `D (X) ≠ 0` in `Ω[Frac(k[X])⁄k]`. Verified route
112	(analogist iter-154): `Ω[Frac(k[X])⁄k]` is the localization of `Ω[k[X]⁄k]`
113	(`KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`, formally-étale localization),
114	so `D X = 0` would give `c • D_{k[X]} X = 0` for some non-zero-divisor `c`;
115	transporting along the `k[X]`-linear iso `polynomialEquiv` (with
116	`polynomialEquiv_D` ↦ `derivative X = 1`) yields `c • 1 = 0`, contradicting
117	`nonZeroDivisors.coe_ne_zero`. -/
118	private lemma _ratfunc_D_X_ne_zero {k : Type*} [Field k] :
119	    _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k (FractionRing (Polynomial k))
120	      (algebraMap (Polynomial k) (FractionRing (Polynomial k)) Polynomial.X) ≠ 0 := by
121	  intro h
122	  have hmap : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map k k (Polynomial k)
123	      (FractionRing (Polynomial k))
124	      (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k (Polynomial k) Polynomial.X) = 0 := by
125	    rw [_root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_D]; exact h
126	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors (Polynomial k))
127	      (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.map k k (Polynomial k)
128	        (FractionRing (Polynomial k))) :=
129	    _root_.KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map k (Polynomial k)
130	      (FractionRing (Polynomial k)) (nonZeroDivisors (Polynomial k))
131	  rw [IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff (nonZeroDivisors (Polynomial k))] at hmap
132	  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hmap
133	  rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hc
134	  have hc1 := congrArg (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.polynomialEquiv (R := k)) hc
135	  rw [map_smul, _root_.KaehlerDifferential.polynomialEquiv_D, Polynomial.derivative_X,
136	    map_zero, smul_eq_mul, mul_one] at hc1
137	  exact nonZeroDivisors.coe_ne_zero c hc1
138	
139	/-- (FT.3 closer) Over an algebraically closed base field `k`, an element of a
140	`k`-algebra field `K` that is algebraic over `k` lies in the image of
141	`algebraMap k K`. Verified route (analogist iter-154): `k⟮b⟯` is finite over `k`
142	(`IntermediateField.adjoin.finiteDimensional`), hence integral, so
143	`IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` makes `algebraMap k k⟮b⟯`
144	surjective; pull the generator back. -/
145	private lemma _algebraic_mem_range {k K : Type*} [Field k] [Field K] [IsAlgClosed k]
146	    [Algebra k K] {b : K} (hb : IsAlgebraic k b) :
147	    b ∈ (algebraMap k K).range := by
148	  haveI : FiniteDimensional k k⟮b⟯ := IntermediateField.adjoin.finiteDimensional hb.isIntegral
149	  haveI : Algebra.IsIntegral k k⟮b⟯ := Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite k k⟮b⟯
150	  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ :=
151	    (IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral (k := k) (K := k⟮b⟯)).surjective
152	      (IntermediateField.AdjoinSimple.gen k b)
153	  exact ⟨c, by
154	    have := congrArg (algebraMap k⟮b⟯ K) hc
155	    rwa [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply, IntermediateField.AdjoinSimple.algebraMap_gen]
156	      at this⟩
157	
158	/-- Algebra-level core (KDM): for `k` an algebraically closed field of
159	characteristic 0 (`[IsAlgClosed k] [CharZero k]`) and `B` a finite-type
160	`k`-algebra that is an integral domain (`[IsDomain B]`), if `b : B` satisfies
161	`D b = 0` in `Ω[B⁄k]` then `b ∈ range (algebraMap k B)`. This is the
162	chart-algebra (β-core ring-side) helper of the iter-144 piece (ii) pivot,
163	formalising the "constants = kernel of the universal derivation" content of
164	the Kähler derivation in characteristic 0.
165	
166	The JOINT `[IsAlgClosed k]` + `[IsDomain B]` hypotheses are essential — the
167	bare `B`-only form is FALSE (counterexamples: `B = k × k` killed by
168	`[IsDomain B]`; `B = ℚ(√2)/ℚ` killed by `[IsAlgClosed k]`).
169	
170	CLOSED iter-154 via the verified single-element / perfect-field /
171	Jacobi–Zariski `H1Cotangent` route (analogist `ftthree-kernel`,
172	`analogies/ftthree-kernel-iter154.md`; blueprint `RigidityKbar.tex` § FT):
173	* (FT.1) push `D_B b = 0` to the fraction field `K = Frac B` via
174	  `KaehlerDifferential.map_D`; it suffices to put `algebraMap B K b` in
175	  `range (algebraMap k K)` and pull back along the injective `B ↪ K`;
176	* (FT.2) reduce to `IsAlgebraic k (algebraMap B K b)`: if it were
177	  transcendental, embed `F = Frac(k[X]) ↪ K` (`X ↦ bK`); char-0 ⟹
178	  `PerfectField F`, and `K` is `EssFiniteType` over `F`, so
179	  `Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField` gives `FormallySmooth F K`, hence
180	  `Subsingleton (H1Cotangent F K)`; the Jacobi–Zariski exact sequence
181	  (`Algebra.H1Cotangent.exact_δ_mapBaseChange`) then makes `mapBaseChange k F K`
182	  injective, and `mapBaseChange (1 ⊗ D_F X_F) = D_K bK = 0` ⟹ (faithful
183	  flatness, `Module.FaithfullyFlat.one_tmul_eq_zero_iff`) `D_F X_F = 0`;
184	* (FT.3) the base case `_ratfunc_D_X_ne_zero` (`D_F X ≠ 0` via the localized
185	  Kähler module + `polynomialEquiv_D`) contradicts that, so `bK` is algebraic;
186	  `_algebraic_mem_range` (alg-closed `k`) then lands `bK ∈ range`.
187	
188	The standard-smooth / relative-dimension hypotheses (`n`,
189	`[IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]`) are NOT used by this route —
190	only `[IsDomain B]` + `[Algebra.FiniteType k B]` to build `K` and the
191	`EssFiniteType k K` chain — but they are retained on the signature (frozen,
192	harmless) for the downstream consumer.
193	
194	Reverse inclusion: `_hRev` records that `D ∘ algebraMap k B = 0` so the
195	forward direction stated here is the substantive content. Downstream
196	consumer: `GrpObj.df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` below. -/
197	theorem KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero
198	    {k : Type u} [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k] [CharZero k]
199	    {B : Type u} [CommRing B] [IsDomain B] [Algebra k B] [Algebra.FiniteType k B]
200	    {n : ℕ} [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]
201	    {b : B} (hDb : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B b = 0) :
202	    b ∈ (algebraMap k B).range := by
203	  classical
204	  -- Reverse inclusion (recorded for downstream symmetry):
205	  -- `D : Derivation k B Ω[B⁄k]` vanishes on the algebraMap image by
206	  -- `Derivation.map_algebraMap`.
207	  have _hRev : ∀ a : k, _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B (algebraMap k B a) = 0 :=
208	    fun a => (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B).map_algebraMap a
209	  -- ===========================================================================
210	  -- iter-154 closure: the verified single-element / perfect-field /
211	  -- Jacobi–Zariski `H1Cotangent` route (analogist `ftthree-kernel`,
212	  -- `analogies/ftthree-kernel-iter154.md`). The standard-smooth structure is
213	  -- NOT used by this argument (only `[IsDomain B]` + `[FiniteType k B]` to
214	  -- build `K = Frac B` and `EssFiniteType k K`); `n` and standard-smoothness
215	  -- remain harmless ambient hypotheses.
216	  -- ===========================================================================
217	  -- (FT.1) Push `D_B b = 0` forward to the fraction field `K = Frac B`.
218	  set K := FractionRing B with hK
219	  have hDbK : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k K (algebraMap B K b) = 0 := by
220	    rw [← _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_D k k B K b, hDb, map_zero]
221	  -- It suffices to put `algebraMap B K b ∈ range(algebraMap k K)`; pull back
222	  -- along the injective `algebraMap B K` (domain ↪ fraction field).
223	  suffices hbK : algebraMap B K b ∈ (algebraMap k K).range by
224	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hbK
225	    refine ⟨c, ?_⟩
226	    apply IsFractionRing.injective B K
227	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply k B K]
228	    exact hc
229	  -- Reduce to algebraicity of `bK := algebraMap B K b` over `k`, then close
230	  -- with `_algebraic_mem_range` (alg-closed `k`).
231	  apply _algebraic_mem_range
232	  -- (FT.2) Suppose, for contradiction, that `bK` is transcendental over `k`.
233	  by_contra hCon
234	  have hTr : Transcendental k (algebraMap B K b) := hCon
235	  have hInj : Function.Injective ⇑(Polynomial.aeval (algebraMap B K b)) :=
236	    transcendental_iff_injective.mp hTr
237	  -- Embed `F = Frac(k[X])` into `K` by `X ↦ bK` via `IsFractionRing.lift`.
238	  set F := FractionRing (Polynomial k) with hF
239	  have hg : Function.Injective ⇑((Polynomial.aeval (algebraMap B K b)).toRingHom) := hInj
240	  letI algFK : Algebra F K := (IsFractionRing.lift hg).toAlgebra
241	  have hψ : ∀ x : Polynomial k,
242	      algebraMap F K (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F x) =
243	        (Polynomial.aeval (algebraMap B K b)).toRingHom x :=
244	    fun x => IsFractionRing.lift_algebraMap hg x
245	  -- Scalar tower `k → F → K`: `algebraMap F K ∘ algebraMap k F = algebraMap k K`.
246	  haveI : IsScalarTower k F K := by
247	    refine IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (fun x => ?_)
248	    rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply k (Polynomial k) F x, hψ]
249	    change algebraMap k K x =
250	      (Polynomial.aeval (algebraMap B K b)) (algebraMap k (Polynomial k) x)
251	    rw [Polynomial.algebraMap_eq, Polynomial.aeval_C]
252	  -- `K` is essentially of finite type over `F` (via `EssFiniteType k K`).
253	  haveI : Algebra.EssFiniteType k K := Algebra.EssFiniteType.comp k B (FractionRing B)
254	  haveI : Algebra.EssFiniteType F K := Algebra.EssFiniteType.of_comp k F K
255	  -- `F` is a perfect field (char 0) ⟹ `K/F` formally smooth ⟹ `H¹` vanishes.
256	  haveI : PerfectField F := PerfectField.ofCharZero
257	  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth F K := Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField
258	  -- (FT.2 core) `mapBaseChange k F K` is injective via the Jacobi–Zariski
259	  -- exact sequence + `Subsingleton (H1Cotangent F K)` from formal smoothness.
260	  have hMapInj : Function.Injective (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange k F K) := by
261	    rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
262	    intro x hx
263	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := (Algebra.H1Cotangent.exact_δ_mapBaseChange k F K x).mp hx
264	    rw [Subsingleton.elim y 0, map_zero]
265	  -- `algebraMap F K (image of X) = bK`.
266	  have hbKeq : algebraMap F K (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X) =
267	      algebraMap B K b := by
268	    rw [hψ]; exact Polynomial.aeval_X _
269	  -- `mapBaseChange (1 ⊗ D_F X_F) = D_K (algebraMap F K X_F) = D_K bK = 0`.
270	  have hident : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange k F K
271	      (1 ⊗ₜ _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k F
272	        (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X)) =
273	      _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k K
274	        (algebraMap F K (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X)) := by
275	    rw [_root_.KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange_tmul, one_smul,
276	      _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_D]
277	  have h1tmul : (1 ⊗ₜ _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k F
278	      (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X) : K ⊗[F] Ω[F⁄k]) = 0 := by
279	    apply hMapInj
280	    rw [map_zero, hident, hbKeq]
281	    exact hDbK
282	  -- Faithful flatness of the field extension `F → K` cancels the `1 ⊗ -`.
283	  have hDFX : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k F
284	      (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X) = 0 :=
285	    (Module.FaithfullyFlat.one_tmul_eq_zero_iff F (Ω[F⁄k]) _).mp h1tmul
286	  -- (FT.3 base case) But `D_F X_F ≠ 0` — contradiction.
287	  exact _ratfunc_D_X_ne_zero hDFX
288	
289	namespace GrpObj
290	
291	/-- Chart-algebra (β-core): per-chart translation-invariance Kähler-derivation.
292	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C/k` and an affine chart
293	`V ⊆ C` whose chart-ring `B = Γ(V, O_C)` carries a finite-type `k`-algebra
294	structure, a chart-section `b ∈ B` with vanishing universal Kähler derivation
295	`KaehlerDifferential.D k B b = 0` lies in `range (algebraMap k B)` — the chart-
296	local kernel-of-`D` content of the iter-144 chart-algebra pivot piece (ii) is
297	the algebra-level statement that constants are the image of the base field.
298	
299	Iter-147 signature refinement: the chart-of-proper-curve hypothesis is carried
300	as typeclass arguments on `C` (smooth, proper, reduced, geometrically
301	irreducible over `Spec k`); the chart-ring `B` is carried as a separate finite-
302	type `k`-algebra argument with the (chart-side identification `B ≃ₐ[k] Γ(V, O_C)`
303	deferred to the consumer site via the `Scheme.Over.ext_of_diff_zero` refinement
304	plan; iter-148+). The body delegates to the algebra-level KDM helper
305	`KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero` above.
306	
307	The full 5-step closure path is documented in `blueprint/src/chapters/
308	RigidityKbar.tex` § "Chart-algebra piece (ii) first-class decomposition"
309	under `lem:chart_algebra_df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`:
310	(1) chart-Kähler kernel extraction (this is KDM); (2) standard-smooth chart
311	witness from `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential`;
312	(3) 2-chart Čech Mayer–Vietoris on `Ω_{C/k}^{⊕n}` reusing
313	`Cohomology/MayerVietoris*`; (4) 2-chart-cover existence per Stacks 0F8L;
314	(5) char-p Frobenius patch via `RingHom.iterateFrobenius_comm`. -/
315	theorem df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart
316	    {k : Type u} [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k] [CharZero k]
317	    {C : Scheme.{u}} [C.Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))]
318	    [IsProper (C ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))]
319	    [Smooth (C ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))]
320	    [IsReduced C]
321	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (C ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))]
322	    {B : Type u} [CommRing B] [IsDomain B] [Algebra k B] [Algebra.FiniteType k B]
323	    {n : ℕ} [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]
324	    {b : B} (hDb : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B b = 0) :
325	    b ∈ (algebraMap k B).range :=
326	  -- Iter-149 signature inflation: `[CharZero k]` and
327	  -- `[Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]` propagated
328	  -- from the KDM (p2) bridge directive in `PROGRESS.md` § "Iter-149
329	  -- Lane 2". Char-p alternative (p1) path is deferred. The scheme-
330	  -- level chart-of-proper-curve hypotheses on `C` remain the
331	  -- standing premise for a future char-p reactivation if the
332	  -- project widens the KDM commitment.
333	  AlgebraicGeometry.KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero (n := n) hDb
334	
335	end GrpObj
336	
337	/-- Integrally-closed-constants helper: in a (smooth) proper geometrically
338	irreducible scheme `X` over a base field `k`, the global sections
339	`Γ(X, O_X)` are exhausted by the image of the structure-morphism's
340	action on the top open, i.e.\ the `appTop` ring hom of the structure
341	morphism `X ⟶ Spec k` is surjective. Equivalently, `Γ(X, O_X) ≅ k` as
342	`k`-algebras (modulo the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≃ k`).
343	
344	Blueprint: `chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
345	`\lem:constants_integral_over_base_field`. CLOSED iter-153 under the
346	iter-152 alg-closed pivot (`[IsAlgClosed k]`); the proof is three steps:
347	(1) `IsReduced X` + `GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec k)` (over the
348	    singleton-base `Spec k`) ⇒ `IrreducibleSpace X`
349	    (`GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`) ⇒
350	    `IsIntegral X` (`isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`);
351	    `IsProper` ⇒ `UniversallyClosed` ⇒ `Γ(X, O_X)` is a field
352	    (`isField_of_universallyClosed`).
353	(2) `IsProper` (⇒ `UniversallyClosed` + `LocallyOfFiniteType`) ⇒ the
354	    structure-morphism `appTop` ring hom `k → Γ(X, O_X)` is finite
355	    (`finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`), hence `Γ(X, O_X)` is
356	    integral over `k`.
357	(3) `k` algebraically closed ⇒ an integral extension is trivial:
358	    `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` gives
359	    `algebraMap k Γ(X, O_X)` bijective, hence surjective, i.e.
360	    `RingHom.range (appTop.hom) = ⊤`. (Under `[IsAlgClosed k]` the
361	    former base-change-to-`\bar k` substep collapses entirely — no
362	    separability/inseparability factorisation is used. The old
363	    `ChartAlgebraS3` route was removed iter-155 as dead scaffolding.)
364	
365	The `[IsReduced X]` hypothesis is added explicitly because Mathlib
366	snapshot `b80f227` lacks the lemma `Smooth ⇒ IsReduced` over a field —
367	see the `Rigidity.lean` "Hypothesis history" block for the same
368	explicit-`IsReduced` discipline. -/
369	theorem constants_integral_over_base_field
370	    {k : Type u} [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k] {X : Scheme.{u}} [X.Over (Spec (.of k))]
371	    [IsProper (X ↘ Spec (.of k))]
372	    [Smooth (X ↘ Spec (.of k))]
373	    [IsReduced X]
374	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec (.of k))] :
375	    RingHom.range ((X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop.hom) = ⊤ := by
376	  -- Iter-153 alg-closed closure. Under `[IsAlgClosed k]` the proof
377	  -- collapses to three steps: (1) `X` integral ⟹ `Γ(X, O_X)` a field;
378	  -- (2) properness ⟹ `Γ(X, O_X)` finite (hence integral) over `k`;
379	  -- (3) `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` gives the
380	  -- structure ring hom `k → Γ(X, O_X)` bijective, hence surjective,
381	  -- which is the displayed `range = ⊤`.
382	  set f := X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) with hf
383	  -- (1) `X` is integral: smoothness gives `IsReduced X`, geometric
384	  -- irreducibility over the singleton base `Spec k` gives `IrreducibleSpace X`,
385	  -- and the two combine to `IsIntegral X`.
386	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace X :=
387	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton f
388	  haveI : IsIntegral X := isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced X
389	  -- (1, cont.) properness ⟹ universally closed ⟹ `Γ(X, ⊤)` is a field.
390	  letI : Field Γ(X, ⊤) := (isField_of_universallyClosed k f).toField
391	  -- (2) The composite `F : k → Γ(X, ⊤)` (structure map through the
392	  -- canonical `ΓSpecIso`) is finite, hence integral over `k`.
393	  let F := (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫ f.appTop
394	  have hFinF : F.hom.Finite := by
395	    apply RingHom.finite_respectsIso.2
396	      (e := (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv)
397	    exact finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k f
398	  letI : Algebra k Γ(X, ⊤) := F.hom.toAlgebra
399	  haveI : Module.Finite k Γ(X, ⊤) := hFinF
400	  haveI : Algebra.IsIntegral k Γ(X, ⊤) := Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite k Γ(X, ⊤)
401	  -- (3) `k` algebraically closed ⟹ `algebraMap k Γ(X, ⊤) = F.hom` is
402	  -- bijective, hence surjective.
403	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap k Γ(X, ⊤)) :=
404	    IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral
405	  -- Convert the range goal to surjectivity of `f.appTop.hom`; since
406	  -- `F.hom = f.appTop.hom ∘ (ΓSpecIso).inv.hom` is surjective, so is
407	  -- `f.appTop.hom`.
408	  rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]
409	  have hFsurj : Function.Surjective ⇑F.hom := hbij.surjective
410	  have hcomp : F.hom = f.appTop.hom.comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom := rfl
411	  rw [hcomp, RingHom.coe_comp] at hFsurj
412	  exact hFsurj.of_comp
413	
414	namespace Scheme
415	
416	namespace Over
417	
418	/-- Scheme-level lift: two morphisms `f, g : C ⟶ A` in `Over (Spec k)`
419	agreeing on a non-empty open subset `U ⊆ C.left` are equal, when `A`
420	is separated over `Spec k`, `C.left` is reduced, and `C` is
421	geometrically irreducible over `Spec k`.
422	
423	This is a thin renaming of the iter-125 packaging
424	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` (in
425	`AlgebraicJacobian/Rigidity.lean`) under the
426	`Scheme.Over.ext_of_diff_zero` name committed by the iter-145
427	chart-algebra planner. Per the blueprint's three-step
428	proof recipe (`chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
429	`\lem:Scheme_Over_ext_of_diff_zero`), the iter-125 packaging
430	discharges Step 3 (the `ext_of_eqOnOpen` packaging) directly given the
431	`eqOnOpen` hypothesis. Iter-147+ will refine the signature to *also*
432	take a chart-algebra `df = dg` hypothesis and *derive* `eqOnOpen` from
433	it via Steps 1–2 of the recipe (chart-algebra
434	`df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` β-core; currently a
435	deferred sub-piece, see L97 above). The lift here is the iter-146
436	sorry-free closure under the planner's chart-algebra envelope; the
437	substantive Steps 1–2 derivation lands once the β-core sub-piece
438	closes.
439	
440	Hypotheses match `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`; the renaming preserves
441	the iter-125 proof discipline that `IsSeparated A.hom` (not the
442	stronger `IsProper`) is sufficient for the rigidity packaging. -/
443	theorem ext_of_diff_zero
444	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
445	    {C A : Over (Spec (.of k))}
446	    [IsSeparated A.hom]
447	    [IsReduced C.left]
448	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
449	    (f g : C ⟶ A) (U : C.left.Opens) (hU : (U : Set C.left).Nonempty)
450	    (hUf : (U.ι : (U : C.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ C.left) ≫ f.left =
451	      (U.ι : (U : C.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ C.left) ≫ g.left) :
452	    f = g :=
453	  AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen f g U hU hUf
454	
455	end Over
456	
457	end Scheme
458	
459	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	/-!
10	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 3): standard `k̄`-points on `ℙ¹`; `𝔾_a` and `𝔾_m`
11	
12	This file is **Stratum 3** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
13	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
14	
15	* the three standard `k̄`-points `0 = [0 : 1]`, `1 = [1 : 1]`, `∞ = [1 : 0]` of
16	  `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, encoded as sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`;
17	* the additive group `Ga` over `Spec k̄` (= `AffineSpace (Fin 1)`) and its
18	  affine, finite-presentation, and reduced instances;
19	* the multiplicative group `Gm` over `Spec k̄` (= `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`) and its
20	  affine, finite-presentation, reduced, domain, and irreducible-space instances,
21	  plus the scaffold `GrpObj Gm` and the resulting `Smooth Gm.hom`;
22	* the multiplicative identity `Gm.onePt = η[Gm kbar]`.
23	
24	Upstream strata: `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`. Downstream stratum: `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
39	
40	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
41	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
42	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
43	
44	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
45	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
46	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
47	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
48	
49	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
50	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
51	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
52	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
53	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
54	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
55	
56	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
57	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
58	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
59	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
60	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
61	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
62	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
63	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
64	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
65	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
66	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
67	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
68	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
69	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
70	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
71	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
72	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
73	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
74	    exact hi.map _
75	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
76	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
77	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
78	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
79	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
80	
81	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
82	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
83	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
84	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
85	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
86	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
87	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
88	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
89	  Over.homMk
90	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
91	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
92	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
93	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
94	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
95	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
96	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
97	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
98	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
99	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
100	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
101	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
102	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
103	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
104	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
105	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
106	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
107	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
108	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
109	      ext r
110	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
111	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
112	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
113	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
114	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
115	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
116	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
117	
118	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
119	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
120	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
121	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
122	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
123	
124	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
125	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
126	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
127	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
128	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
129	
130	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
131	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
132	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
133	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
134	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
135	
136	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
137	
138	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
139	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
140	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
141	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
142	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
143	
144	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
145	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
146	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
147	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
148	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
149	
150	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
151	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
152	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
153	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
154	
155	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
156	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
157	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
158	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
159	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
160	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
161	
162	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
163	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
164	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
165	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
166	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
167	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
168	
169	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
170	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
171	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
172	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
173	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
174	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
175	
176	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
177	
178	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
179	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
180	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
181	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
182	
183	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
184	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
185	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
186	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
187	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
188	
189	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
190	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
191	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
192	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
193	
194	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
195	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
196	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
197	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
198	
199	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
200	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
201	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
202	
203	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
204	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
205	element is finitely presented), bridged through
206	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
207	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
208	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
209	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
210	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
211	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
212	      inferInstance)
213	
214	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
215	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
216	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
217	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
218	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
219	
220	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
221	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
222	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
223	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
224	
225	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
226	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
227	  unfold GmRing
228	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
229	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
230	
231	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
232	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
233	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
234	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
235	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
236	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
237	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
238	  infer_instance
239	
240	/-! ### `gm_grpObj` — `GrpObj` instance on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`
241	
242	The route is per the iter-179 `gm-grpobj-representable` analogy (8-step recipe).
243	
244	Step A: build the units-of-global-sections functor `gmHomFunctor : (Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat`,
245	which sends `T ↦ Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents.
246	
247	Step B: build the `RepresentableBy` witness asserting `Hom(-, Gm) ≃ Γ(-, ⊤)ˣ` naturally.
248	The per-`T` bijection is the 3-step chain (over-cat unfold → Γ-Spec adjunction →
249	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` universal property of `k̄[t, t⁻¹]`).
250	
251	Step C: `gm_grpObj := GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) gmHomFunctor _`. -/
252	
253	/-- The `(Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u}` functor of units of global sections.
254	This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents. -/
255	private noncomputable def gmHomFunctor (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
256	    (Over (Spec (.of kbar)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u} where
257	  obj T := GrpCat.of (Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤))ˣ
258	  map {_ _} φ := GrpCat.ofHom (Units.map (φ.unop.left.appTop).hom.toMonoidHom)
259	  map_id := by
260	    intro T
261	    apply GrpCat.hom_ext
262	    apply MonoidHom.ext
263	    intro u
264	    apply Units.ext
265	    change ((𝟙 T.unop : T.unop ⟶ T.unop).left.appTop).hom u.val = u.val
266	    rw [Over.id_left]
267	    simp
268	  map_comp := by
269	    intros T T' T'' φ ψ
270	    apply GrpCat.hom_ext
271	    apply MonoidHom.ext
272	    intro u
273	    apply Units.ext
274	    change ((φ ≫ ψ).unop.left.appTop).hom u.val
275	      = ((ψ.unop.left.appTop).hom (((φ.unop.left.appTop).hom) u.val))
276	    rw [show (φ ≫ ψ).unop = ψ.unop ≫ φ.unop from rfl, Over.comp_left]
277	    simp
278	
279	/-- **Forward** of the per-`T` bijection: a morphism `f : T ⟶ Gm kbar` in `Over (Spec k̄)`
280	gives the unit in `Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ` corresponding to the image of the standard generator
281	`t ∈ k̄[t, t⁻¹]` under the induced ring map.
282	
283	Concretely, `f.left.appTop : Γ(Spec(GmRing), ⊤) ⟶ Γ(T.left, ⊤)`; composing with
284	`ΓSpecIso.inv` gives a ring map `GmRing → Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. The image of the algebra-map
285	generator `t ∈ GmRing` (a unit via `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`) is a unit
286	in `Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. -/
287	private noncomputable def gmHomEquiv_toFun (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
288	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (f : T ⟶ Gm kbar) : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ :=
289	  letI ringMap : (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)) ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
290	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
291	  Units.map ringMap.hom.toMonoidHom
292	    (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit
293	      (S := GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)).unit
294	
295	/-- Helper for `gmHomEquiv_invFun`: the underlying scheme morphism factors over `Spec k̄`.
296	Substantive content: reduces to `IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp` + `MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq`
297	+ `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` (the Γ⊣Spec unit triangle). -/
298	private lemma gmHomEquiv_invFun_isOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
299	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (u : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ) :
300	    letI structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
301	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
302	    letI polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
303	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val)
304	    haveI hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
305	      change IsUnit (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()))
306	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
307	      exact u.isUnit
308	    letI liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
309	      IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
310	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg
311	    (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap)) ≫ (Gm kbar).hom = T.hom := by
312	  -- Set up local abbreviations matching the letI's of the goal.
313	  set structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
314	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
315	  set polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
316	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val) with polyMap_def
317	  have hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
318	    rw [polyMap_def, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
319	    exact u.isUnit
320	  set liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
321	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
322	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg with liftedMap_def
323	  -- Unfold `(Gm kbar).hom`.
324	  change T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap) ≫
325	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) = T.hom
326	  -- Combine the Spec.maps.
327	  rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
328	  -- `liftedMap ∘ algebraMap kbar GmRing = structureRingMap.hom`.
329	  have halg : (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) =
330	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)).comp
331	        (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
332	    ext r
333	    exact (IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) r).symm
334	  have hcomp : liftedMap.comp (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) = structureRingMap.hom := by
335	    change (IsLocalization.Away.lift _ hg).comp _ = _
336	    rw [halg, ← RingHom.comp_assoc, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp]
337	    ext r
338	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ ((algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) r) = _
339	    rw [MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
340	  rw [hcomp]
341	  -- Now: T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map structureRingMap.hom = T.hom
342	  -- structureRingMap = ΓSpecIso.inv ≫ T.hom.appTop  (as CommRingCat morphism)
343	  change T.left.toSpecΓ ≫
344	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫
345	          T.hom.appTop).hom)) = T.hom
346	  -- CommRingCat.ofHom (...).hom = the original morphism (since ofHom and .hom are inverses)
347	  rw [show CommRingCat.ofHom (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫
348	        T.hom.appTop).hom) = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop from
349	        CommRingCat.ofHom_hom _]
350	  rw [Spec.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality]
351	  -- Now: (T.hom ≫ (Spec _).toSpecΓ) ≫ Spec.map (ΓSpecIso.inv) = T.hom
352	  rw [Category.assoc, toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv, Category.comp_id]
353	
354	/-- **Backward** of the per-`T` bijection: a unit `u : Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ` gives the morphism
355	`T ⟶ Gm kbar` defined by the ring map `k̄[t, t⁻¹] → Γ(T.left, ⊤)` sending `t ↦ u.val`.
356	
357	Concretely: build the polynomial-ring map `MvPolynomial Unit k̄ →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤)` via
358	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` (sending `X() ↦ u.val`); lift through the localization via
359	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` (using that `u.val` is a unit); convert to a scheme morphism
360	via `Spec.map`-then-`toSpecΓ`; bundle with the `Over`-commutativity proof. -/
361	private noncomputable def gmHomEquiv_invFun (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
362	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (u : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ) : T ⟶ Gm kbar :=
363	  -- structure ring map kbar → Γ(T.left, ⊤), via T.hom.appTop and ΓSpecIso
364	  letI structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
365	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
366	  letI polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
367	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val)
368	  haveI hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
369	    change IsUnit (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()))
370	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
371	    exact u.isUnit
372	  letI liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
373	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
374	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg
375	  Over.homMk
376	    (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap))
377	    (gmHomEquiv_invFun_isOver kbar T u)
378	
379	/-- Round-trip identity 1: `invFun ∘ toFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
380	the Γ⊣Spec adjunction injectivity (`ext_to_Spec`) + `IsLocalization.Away.lift` uniqueness
381	applied to the lifted ring map from the morphism's `appTop`. -/
382	private lemma gmHomEquiv_left_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
383	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
384	    Function.LeftInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
385	  intro f
386	  apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
387	  -- Goal: (invFun (toFun f)).left = f.left as T.left ⟶ Spec(GmRing).
388	  -- (a) `Over.w f` transferred through `Spec.appTop` + `ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality`:
389	  --     `structureRingMap = algebraMap kbar GmRing ≫ g_f` (as CommRingCat morphisms),
390	  --     where `g_f := (ΓSpecIso (GmRing)).inv ≫ f.left.appTop`.
391	  have hw : (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop =
392	      CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) ≫
393	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop) := by
394	    have hOver := Over.w f
395	    -- `(Gm kbar).hom = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))` by defeq.
396	    change f.left ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) = T.hom
397	      at hOver
398	    rw [← hOver, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
399	    set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
400	      rw [← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc]
401	  -- (b) Compare the two morphisms `T.left ⟶ Spec(GmRing)` via `ext_to_Spec`.
402	  apply AlgebraicGeometry.ext_to_Spec
403	  change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
404	      (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T f)).left.appTop
405	    = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
406	  -- Reduce LHS to `CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap_{toFun f}` (same chain as in `right_inv`).
407	  unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun
408	  change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
409	      (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (IsLocalization.Away.lift
410	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) _))).appTop
411	    = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
412	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc,
413	    Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
414	  -- Goal: `CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap_{toFun f} = g_f` as CommRingCat morphisms.
415	  apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
416	  simp only [CommRingCat.hom_ofHom, IsLocalization.Away.lift]
417	  -- Apply `IsLocalization.lift_unique`: it suffices to show
418	  -- `g_f.hom ∘ algebraMap_{MvPoly→GmRing} = polyMap` on the MvPoly side.
419	  refine IsLocalization.lift_unique _ ?_
420	  change ∀ (x : MvPolynomial Unit kbar),
421	    (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop).hom.comp
422	        (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))) x = (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _) x
423	  apply DFunLike.congr_fun
424	  -- Both sides are RingHoms `MvPoly Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. By `ringHom_ext`,
425	  -- it suffices to compare on `C r` (constants) and `X i` (variables).
426	  refine MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext (fun r => ?_) (fun i => ?_)
427	  · -- `C r`-case: by `Over.w f`, `g_f ∘ algebraMap_{kbar→GmRing} = structureRingMap`.
428	    have hwr := congrArg (fun (g : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ _) => g.hom r) hw
429	    simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply, CommRingCat.hom_ofHom] at hwr
430	    simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
431	    -- Now LHS = g_f.hom (algebraMap kbar GmRing r), RHS = structureRingMap.hom r
432	    exact hwr.symm
433	  · -- `X i`-case: `i : Unit`, so `i = ()`; then both sides reduce to `(toFun f).val`.
434	    obtain ⟨⟩ := i
435	    simp [gmHomEquiv_toFun]
436	
437	/-- Round-trip identity 2: `toFun ∘ invFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
438	`IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` (image of the algebra-map generator is the unit)
439	plus `ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality` + `Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop` (canceling the unit triangle). -/
440	private lemma gmHomEquiv_right_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
441	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
442	    Function.RightInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
443	  intro u
444	  apply Units.ext
445	  -- Stage the proof at the CommRingCat morphism level.
446	  change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
447	        (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T u).left.appTop).hom
448	      ((algebraMap _ _) (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) = u.val
449	  -- Unfold `invFun` to expose `T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (ofHom liftedMap)` as the
450	  -- underlying scheme morphism.
451	  unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun
452	  change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
453	      (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
454	        (IsLocalization.Away.lift
455	          (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) _))).appTop).hom
456	      ((algebraMap _ _) (MvPolynomial.X ())) = u.val
457	  -- Step (a): morphism equality `(ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ invFun(u).left.appTop = ofHom liftedMap`
458	  -- via `Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`, `← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc`,
459	  -- `Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop`, `Iso.inv_hom_id`, `Category.comp_id`.
460	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc,
461	    Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
462	  -- Step (b)+(c): conclude via `IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` + `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X'`.
463	  simp [IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
464	
465	/-- Naturality of `homEquiv`: pre-composition by `φ : T' ⟶ T` in `Over (Spec k̄)` corresponds
466	to `Units.map` of `φ.left.appTop` on the units side. Reduces to `Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`
467	and the unit-element transfer through `IsLocalization.Away.lift` and the functor's `map` action. -/
468	private lemma gmHomEquiv_homEquiv_comp (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
469	    {T T' : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} (f : T ⟶ T') (g : T' ⟶ Gm kbar) :
470	    gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T (f ≫ g) =
471	      (gmHomFunctor kbar ⋙ forget _).map f.op (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T' g) := by
472	  apply Units.ext
473	  simp only [gmHomEquiv_toFun, gmHomFunctor, Functor.comp_map, Units.coe_map,
474	    IsUnit.unit_spec, GrpCat.hom_ofHom, Over.comp_left, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
475	  rfl
476	
477	/-- The `RepresentableBy` witness exhibiting `Gm kbar` as a representing object for the
478	units-of-global-sections functor. Per-`T` bijection is `gmHomEquiv_toFun` / `gmHomEquiv_invFun`;
479	round-trip identities split into `gmHomEquiv_left_inv` / `gmHomEquiv_right_inv` (named
480	substantive sorries) and naturality `homEquiv_comp` (named substantive sorry). The deep
481	Mathlib-gap content per the iter-179 `gm-grpobj-representable` analogy — no
482	`Spec(Localization.Away)` analogue of `AffineSpace.homOverEquiv` in Mathlib. -/
483	private noncomputable def gmHomFunctor_representableBy (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
484	    (gmHomFunctor kbar ⋙ forget _).RepresentableBy (Gm kbar) where
485	  homEquiv {T} :=
486	    { toFun := gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T
487	      invFun := gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T
488	      left_inv := gmHomEquiv_left_inv kbar T
489	      right_inv := gmHomEquiv_right_inv kbar T }
490	  homEquiv_comp := gmHomEquiv_homEquiv_comp kbar
491	
492	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
493	
494	Installs the multiplicative-group structure on `Gm` using `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with
495	the units functor `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. The representable-by witness exploits the
496	fact that morphisms into `Spec (Localization.Away t)` correspond exactly to units in the
497	global sections (Mathlib's `IsLocalization.Away`-Spec bijection).
498	
499	This `GrpObj Gm` is the LIVE consumer of the iter-166 `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`
500	proof body (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut applies `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` with
501	`W = Gm`). -/
502	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) :=
503	  GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) (gmHomFunctor kbar) (gmHomFunctor_representableBy kbar)
504	
505	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once
506	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
507	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
508	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=
509	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Gm kbar).hom) := gm_grpObj kbar
510	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Gm kbar).hom
511	
512	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (the multiplicative identity), encoded as the group-object
513	unit `η[Gm kbar] : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm kbar`. -/
514	def Gm.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
515	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Gm kbar :=
516	  η[Gm kbar]
517	
518	end AlgebraicGeometry
519	
520	end

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.BareScheme
7	
8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 2): the chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`
10	
11	This file is **Stratum 2** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships the
13	load-bearing chart-ring iso
14	
15	```
16	HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X i)
17	  ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar
18	```
19	
20	together with the `kbar`-algebra preservation lemma. The forward direction is via
21	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`; the inverse via
22	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i = isLocalizationElem`.
23	
24	Upstream stratum: `BareScheme`. Downstream strata: `Points`, `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The chart-ring iso: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]` -/
39	
40	/-- **The "other" `Fin 2` index** used in the chart-`i` affine coordinate `X (other i) / X i`. -/
41	def otherFin : Fin 2 → Fin 2
42	  | 0 => 1
43	  | 1 => 0
44	
45	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_zero : otherFin 0 = 1 := rfl
46	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_one : otherFin 1 = 0 := rfl
47	
48	private lemma otherFin_ne (i : Fin 2) : otherFin i ≠ i := by
49	  fin_cases i <;> decide
50	
51	/-- **The chart-`i` evaluation `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄ →+* MvPolynomial Unit k̄`**: sends
52	`X i ↦ 1` and `X (otherFin i) ↦ X ()`. -/
53	private noncomputable def chartEvalRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
54	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
55	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))
56	    (fun j : Fin 2 => if j = i then (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) else MvPolynomial.X ())
57	
58	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_self (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
59	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X i) = 1 := by
60	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]

13	This file ships **substrate** lemmas needed to close the Lane B

15	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`) together with two off-target sorries

21	follows the recipe of `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` (§2) and the prover-facing
22	specification at `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`.
23	
24	## Contents (this iter)
25	
26	* `AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` —
27	  Substrate 1 (iter-189, axiom-clean): the closed-immersion universal
28	  property phrased as a topological range containment (under reduced
29	  source / target hypotheses).

32	  Substrate 2 (iter-190): the tensor
33	  `Away f ⊗_{k̄} GmRing k̄` is a domain, via the iso chain to

53	
54	/--
55	**Closed immersion lift by range inclusion.**
56	For a closed immersion `i : Z ⟶ X` whose source `Z` is reduced and a
57	quasi-compact morphism `g : Y ⟶ X` whose source `Y` is reduced, `g` factors
58	through `i` if and only if the topological range of `g` is contained in the
59	topological range of `i`.

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
7	
8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`; the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all
17	project from the witness directly.
18	
19	This file currently contains TWO `sorry`-bodied declarations (the Phase-C scaffolding
20	inventory):
21	
22	1. `genusZeroWitness` (iter-127 scaffold): a `JacobianWitness C` for a genus-`0` curve,
23	   with underlying scheme `Spec k`. The body is a `sorry` to be closed once pieces
24	   (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile land (iter-138+); the
25	   substantive mathematical content is the genus-`0` rigidity statement
26	   `rigidity_over_kbar` (M2.a; `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`).
27	2. `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (Phase-C OFF-LIMITS sorry): existence of an Albanese
28	   witness for an arbitrary smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve; gated on
29	   M2 + M3 closure (Albanese existence in genus `≥ 1` plus genus-`0` rigidity).
30	
31	The file contains:
32	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
33	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
34	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
35	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
36	- `genusZeroWitness`: an Albanese witness for a genus-`0` curve, body `sorry`
37	  (iter-127 scaffold; closure deferred to iter-138+).
38	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: existence of an Albanese witness for every curve —
39	  the headline Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and
40	  genus-`0` rigidity.
41	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
42	  Albanese witness.
43	
44	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
45	
46	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
47	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
48	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
49	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
50	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
51	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
52	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
53	-/
54	
55	set_option autoImplicit false
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
60

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK
8	
9	/-!
10	# Genus of a smooth proper curve
11	
12	The genus of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field.
13	
14	## Status (closed iteration 011 — `genus`)
15	
16	The iter-011 refactor added the `noncomputable` modifier (user-authorised
17	`2026-05-07`) and an import of `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK`
18	so the body uses the project's `ModuleCat k`-flavoured cohomology
19	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009) of the structure sheaf
20	`Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` (iter-006). The body is the probe-confirmed
21	one-liner
22	
23	    Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
24	
25	which is the honest mathematical definition `dim_k H^1(C, O_C)`.
26	
27	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus.tex` for the informal proof sketch and
28	`.archon/STRATEGY.md` for the multi-phase build-out plan.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	/-- The genus of a smooth proper curve. -/
40	noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
41	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
42	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=
43	  Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
44	
45	end AlgebraicGeometry
